option.name: filePath
option.value: /AGENTS (snapshot).md

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
# AGENTS.md вҖ” Guide for AI coding agents
Purpose: help automated agents quickly understand, modify, and verify the FakePlayerPlugin codebase.
1) Quick repo map - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/` вҖ” main code and entry points - `src/main/resources/` вҖ” default `config.yml`, `plugin.yml`, `bot-names.yml`, `bot-messages.yml` - `src/main/resources/language/en.yml` вҖ” all user-facing messages (loaded by `Lang.java`) - `pom.xml` вҖ” build + jar metadata (`target/fpp-*.jar` produced) - `frontend/`, root `package.json`, and `vercel.json` вҖ” separate Node/Vercel status + update API sidecar used by the pluginвҖҷs update-check fallback; not part of the Maven plugin jar build. - `wiki/` вҖ” feature docs (Commands.md, Configuration.md, Getting-Started.md, Database.md, Language.md, Permissions.md, Skin-System.md, Swap-System.md, Bot-Behaviour.md, Bot-Messages.md, Bot-Names.md, Fake-Chat.md, FAQ.md, Migration.md, Home.md, Proxy-Support.md, Config-Sync.md, Placeholders.md) - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/gui/` вҖ” in-game settings GUI (`SettingGui`): 3-row chest that lets admins toggle booleans and set numeric/double config values without editing `config.yml` directly. Opened via `/fpp settings`. Implements `Listener` and is registered in `onEnable` via `registerEvents(settingGui, this)`. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/util/` вҖ” helper subsystems and utilities (e.g. `FppMetrics`, `FppLogger`, `FppPlaceholderExpansion`, `TabListManager`, `BackupManager`, `UpdateChecker`, `ConfigValidator`, `DataMigrator`, `TextUtil`, `ConfigMigrator`, `LuckPermsHelper`, `CompatibilityChecker`, `YamlFileSyncer`, `BotTabTeam`, `WorldGuardHelper`). - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/listener/` вҖ” Bukkit event listeners (`PlayerJoinListener`, `FakePlayerEntityListener`, `FakePlayerKickListener`, `BotCollisionListener`, `PlayerWorldChangeListener`, `ServerListListener`, `BotCommandBlocker`, `BotSpawnProtectionListener`, `BotXpPickupListener`). Note: `LuckPermsUpdateListener` was removed вҖ” LP now detects NMS bots as real players automatically. `BotCommandBlocker` prevents bots from executing any commands. `BotSpawnProtectionListener` prevents lobby/spawn plugins from teleporting bots during their 5-tick spawn grace period. `BotXpPickupListener` gates XP orb pickup (`PlayerPickupExperienceEvent`) and programmatic XP grants (`PlayerExpChangeEvent`) for bots via `Config.bodyPickUpXp()` and the `XpCommand` post-collection cooldown. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/fakeplayer/` вҖ” bot domain objects and subsystems (e.g. `FakePlayer`, `FakePlayerBody`, `FakePlayerManager`, `BotPersistence`, `ChunkLoader`, `SkinRepository`, `SkinFetcher`, `SkinProfile`, `SkinProfileInjector`, `NmsHelper`, `NmsPlayerSpawner`, `NmsReflection`, `BotSwapAI`, `BotChatAI`, `BotBroadcast`, `PacketHelper`, `RemoteBotCache`, `RemoteBotEntry`, `BotType`, `BotCombatMode`, `BotPvpAI`, `PeakHoursManager`, `BotPathfinder`). **BotSwapAI rejoin lifecycle (v1.5.17 critical fix):** rejoin task is now registered AFTER `delete()` completes so `cancel(uuid)` never silently kills the rejoin timer вҖ” bots actually come back. New config: `swap.min-online` (floor that prevents swaps if removing one would drop below), `swap.retry-rejoin` (auto-retry failed rejoins, default `true`), `swap.retry-delay` (seconds before retry, default `60`). Bot identification on rejoin: same-name rejoins use `getByName()` (O(1) `nameIndex` lookup); random-name rejoins use UUID diff. Sub-package `fakeplayer/network/` contains the fake Netty layer: `FakeChannel` (discard channel used by `FakeConnection`), `FakeChannelPipeline` (no-op pipeline), `FakeConnection` (NMS `Connection` subclass вҖ” overrides all three `send()` variants as no-ops to prevent vanilla connection side-effects during `placeNewPlayer()` that previously caused a phantom "Anonymous User" / UUID-0 ghost entry; `isConnected()` always returns `true`; injects `FakeChannel` and calls `Connection.configureSerialization()` on the no-op pipeline to satisfy NMS internal checks), and `FakeServerGamePacketListenerImpl` (no-op SGPL subclass that discards all outbound packets except knockback). Note: `BotHeadAI` has been removed; head-AI functionality (player tracking, look-range, turn-speed) is now driven by config keys `head-ai.*` and executed inside `FakePlayerManager`. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/messaging/` вҖ” proxy communication (`VelocityChannel`): registers `fpp:main` plugin-messaging channel; wraps outbound messages in a BungeeCord `Forward ALL` envelope so Velocity/BungeeCord re-delivers to every backend. Subchannels: `BOT_SPAWN`, `BOT_DESPAWN`, `BOT_UPDATE` (display-name sync вҖ” wire: `[msgId][serverId][uuid][newDisplayName]`; sent by `broadcastBotDisplayNameUpdate(fp)` when LP refreshes a bot's display name), `CHAT`, `ALERT`, `JOIN`, `LEAVE`, `SYNC`. Echo suppression via per-message unique IDs. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/sync/` вҖ” proxy config sync (`ConfigSyncManager`): push/pull config files to/from MySQL in NETWORK mode. Supports `DISABLED`, `MANUAL`, `AUTO_PULL`, `AUTO_PUSH` modes. Server-specific keys (`database.server-id`, `database.mysql.*`, `debug`) are never synced. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/database/` вҖ” session history and analytics (`DatabaseManager`, `BotRecord`). - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/lang/` вҖ” message rendering (`Lang`). - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/permission/` вҖ” centralised permission registry (`Perm`).
2) Where to start (entry points) - `FakePlayerPlugin.java` вҖ” plugin lifecycle (`onEnable` / `onDisable`) and orchestration. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/command/` вҖ” command classes (`SpawnCommand`, `DeleteCommand` (registers as `despawn`), `ListCommand`, `HelpCommand`, `InfoCommand`, `ChatCommand`, `SwapCommand`, `ReloadCommand`, `FreezeCommand`, `StatsCommand`, `MigrateCommand`, `TpCommand`, `TphCommand`, `LpInfoCommand`, `RankCommand`, `AlertCommand`, `SyncCommand`, `PeaksCommand`, `SettingCommand`, `InventoryCommand`, `MoveCommand`, `XpCommand`, `CmdCommand`, `MineCommand`) - `command/FppCommand.java` вҖ” interface every sub-command implements (`getName`, `getAliases` (default `emptyList()` вҖ” used e.g. by `InventoryCommand` for alias `"inv"`), `getUsage`, `getDescription`, `getPermission`, `canUse`, `execute`, `tabComplete`). - `config/Config.java`, `util/ConfigMigrator.java` вҖ” configuration pattern and automatic migration. - `fakeplayer/FakePlayerManager.java`, `fakeplayer/BotPersistence.java` вҖ” bot lifecycle, persistence and restore. Key new helpers: `requestNavJump(UUID)` вҖ” sets a 5-tick jump-hold for navigation (used by `MoveCommand` to prevent the swim-AI from cancelling jumps before physics fires); `clearNavJump(UUID)` вҖ” clears the pending jump on despawn. - `fakeplayer/BotPathfinder.java` вҖ” server-side A* grid pathfinder for bot navigation (used by `MoveCommand`). Supports 6 `MoveType` actions: `WALK`, `ASCEND`, `DESCEND`, `PARKOUR`, `BREAK`, `PLACE`. Feature flags passed via `PathOptions` record (`parkour`, `breakBlocks`, `placeBlocks`); all default `false`. Key limits: `MAX_NODES=2000` (plain), `MAX_NODES_EXTENDED=4000` (with advanced options), `MAX_RANGE=64` blocks straight-line. Public entry points: `BotPathfinder.findPathMoves(world, sx,sy,sz, tx,ty,tz, opts)` вҶ’ `List<Move>`; `BotPathfinder.findPath(...)` вҶ’ `List<Pos>` (backward-compat, strips action metadata). `walkable(world, x, y, z)` and `passable(world, x, y, z)` are public world-query helpers. - `command/InventoryCommand.java` вҖ” `/fpp inventory <bot>` (alias `inv`). Opens a 54-slot double-chest GUI displaying the bot's full inventory (main storage rows 1-3, hotbar row 4, label bar row 5, equipment+offhand row 6). Implements both `FppCommand` and `Listener`. Equipment slots enforce type restrictions (boots/leggings/chestplate-or-elytra/helmet/offhand). Right-clicking a bot entity directly opens the GUI without a command. `openGui(Player, FakePlayer)` is the public entry point. Registered as a listener in `onEnable` immediately after command registration. Permission: `Perm.INVENTORY = "fpp.inventory"`. - `command/MoveCommand.java` вҖ” `/fpp move <bot> <player>`. Navigates a bot to an online player using `BotPathfinder`. 1-tick repeating task per active navigation; handles WALK/ASCEND/DESCEND/PARKOUR/BREAK/PLACE waypoints. Recalculates path when target moves >3.5 blocks or every 60 ticks. Calls `manager.requestNavJump(UUID)` for ascend/parkour/stuck-recovery jumps. Stuck detection (8 ticks without movement) forces jump + recalc. `cancelAll()` is a public method but is NOT called in `onDisable` (nav tasks cancel themselves on arrival or when the bot/target goes offline). Permission: `Perm.MOVE = "fpp.move"`. - `command/XpCommand.java` вҖ” `/fpp xp <bot>`. Transfers all XP (`getTotalExperience()`) from a bot to the command sender (player only; console gets nothing but XP is still cleared). Clears bot's total XP, level, and progress. Imposes a 30-second post-collection cooldown on the bot's XP pickup (`BotXpPickupListener` reads `plugin.getXpCommand().isOnXpCooldown(uuid)`). Exposed via `plugin.getXpCommand()`. Permission: `Perm.XP = "fpp.user.xp"` (user-tier, included in `fpp.user.*`). - `fakeplayer/PeakHoursManager.java` вҖ” time-window-based bot pool scheduler. Runs a 60-second repeating tick; computes a target fraction of the total bot pool that should be online based on the current time window (`peak-hours.schedule` / `peak-hours.day-overrides`), then gradually sleeps (quietly removes) or wakes (respawns) AFK bots to match that fraction. Stagger spread is configured via `peak-hours.stagger-seconds`. Requires `swap.enabled: true`. Integrates with `BotSwapAI` вҖ” swapped-out bots count toward the pool so short swap absences don't distort the math. Sleeping-bot state is persisted crash-safely via `fpp_sleeping_bots` DB table (populated on every queue mutation; cleared on `wakeAll()`). Crash recovery: `restoreSleepingBotsFromDatabase(db)` repopulates the in-memory deque at startup. Hot-reload: `reload()` wakes all sleeping bots then restarts the tick if still enabled. Key public API: `start()`, `shutdown()`, `reload()`, `wakeAll()`, `wakeBotByName(name)`, `putBotToSleepByName(name)`, `forceCheck()`, `getSleepingCount()`, `getTotalPool()`, `getSleepingEntries()`, `getSleepingNames()`, `isSleeping(name)`, `getCurrentWindowLabel()`, `computeTargetFraction()`, `getSecondsToNextWindow()`, `getNextWindowFraction()`, `isRunning()`. Accessed via `plugin.getPeakHoursManager()`. Inner record: `PeakHoursManager.SleepingBot(name, loc)`. - `fakeplayer/FakePlayer.java` вҖ” domain object for a single active bot (name, UUID, profile, spawnLocation, displayName, skinName, frozen state). `getPhysicsEntity()` and `getPlayer()` both return the single NMS ServerPlayer entity. Additional fields: `luckpermsGroup` (per-bot LP group override, `null` = not yet resolved; get/set via `getLuckpermsGroup()`/`setLuckpermsGroup()`), `rawDisplayName` (the `{bot_name}` slot before LP prefix/suffix are added; used as the stable base for `refreshLpDisplayName` rebuilds), `bodyless` (when `true` the bot exists in tab-list/chat only, no entity in world вҖ” used for console spawns without location data), `botType` (`BotType` enum вҖ” `AFK` = default passive bot; `PVP` = PvP bot (name prefixed `pvp_<name>`, currently restricted to internal dev UUID); get/set via `getBotType()`/`setBotType()`), `chatEnabled` (per-bot chat mute flag, default `true`; `false` = permanently silenced; get/set via `isChatEnabled()`/`setChatEnabled()`), `chatTier` (per-bot chat activity tier override, `null` = random; values `"quiet"`, `"passive"`, `"normal"`, `"active"`, `"chatty"`; get/set via `getChatTier()`/`setChatTier()`). Note: `botIndex` has been removed; `nametagEntity` remains as a legacy-compat field (no-op for NMS players). - `fakeplayer/FakePlayerBody.java` вҖ” NMS ServerPlayer spawning/removal. Entities tagged via PDC keys for identification. - `fakeplayer/NmsPlayerSpawner.java` вҖ” reflection-based NMS `ServerPlayer` factory (cached class/method/constructor lookups); bypasses Mojang auth by creating the player directly in JVM memory. - `fakeplayer/NmsReflection.java` вҖ” shared NMS reflection bootstrap (CraftBukkit/NMS class resolution); initialised once, thread-safe. - `fakeplayer/RemoteBotCache.java` вҖ” thread-safe registry of bots physically running on other proxy servers. Always initialised (even in LOCAL mode). Populated from DB at startup (NETWORK mode) and updated via `BOT_SPAWN`/`BOT_DESPAWN` plugin messages. Exposed via `FakePlayerPlugin.getRemoteBotCache()`. - `fakeplayer/RemoteBotEntry.java` вҖ” immutable record (`serverId`, `uuid`, `name`, `displayName`, `packetProfileName`, `skinValue`, `skinSignature`) for a remote bot; `hasSkin()` checks whether texture data is present. - `fakeplayer/SkinProfile.java` вҖ” immutable snapshot of a resolved Mojang skin (base64 value + signature + source label). - `config/BotNameConfig.java`, `config/BotMessageConfig.java` вҖ” bot name & message pools which are initialised in `onEnable`. - `fakeplayer/SkinRepository.java` / `fakeplayer/SkinFetcher.java` вҖ” skin-loading subsystem (lazy singleton; explicitly initialised via `SkinRepository.get().init(this)` in `onEnable` after BotMessageConfig but before database init; must be ready before any bot spawn including persistence restore). - `fakeplayer/network/FakeConnection.java` вҖ” NMS `Connection` subclass used for every bot. Created before `placeNewPlayer()` via `new FakeConnection(loopbackAddress)`; sets `this.channel = new FakeChannel(...)` and calls `Connection.configureSerialization()` on the no-op pipeline. Overrides all three `send()` overloads as no-ops to eliminate vanilla connection side-effects (protocol-state registers, pending-ack queues, callback notifications) that caused a phantom "Anonymous User" entry (UUID `0000вҖҰ0000`) in the player list. `isConnected()` always returns `true` so NMS never treats the bot as disconnected. - `fakeplayer/network/FakeServerGamePacketListenerImpl.java` вҖ” extends NMS `ServerGamePacketListenerImpl`; `send()` is a no-op that discards all outbound packets. Exceptions: `ClientboundSetEntityMotionPacket` (knockback) is intercepted and applied server-side via reflected `getMovement()`/`lerpMotion()` (1.21.9+) or `getXa()/Ya()/Za()` fallback. Injected into `ServerPlayer.connection` immediately after `placeNewPlayer()` so `awaitingPositionFromClient` stays `null` вҖ” preventing the server from snapping the bot back to its spawn position. - `fakeplayer/SkinProfileInjector.java` вҖ” reflection helper that injects a `textures` property into an authlib `GameProfile`; used by both NMS body spawns and tab-list packet profiles for consistent skin application. Call `SkinProfileInjector.apply(gameProfile, skinProfile)`. - `fakeplayer/BotIdentityCache.java` вҖ” persistent registry mapping `(bot_name, server_id) вҶ’ stable UUID`. Ensures a bot with the same name always rejoins with the same UUID (LuckPerms data, inventory, session history stay linked). Storage priority: (1) in-memory `ConcurrentHashMap` (always checked first), (2) `fpp_bot_identities` DB table when DB is enabled, (3) `data/bot-identities.yml` YAML fallback when DB is disabled. Initialized in `onEnable` after the DB, set on `FakePlayerManager` via `setIdentityCache()`; `FakePlayerManager.resolveUuid(name)` delegates here for every fresh spawn. `prime(name, uuid)` pre-populates the cache from `spawnRestored()` so restart-restored bots don't hit the DB a second time. **Identity scope:** per-server вҖ” cross-server UUID sharing is intentionally not supported. Keys are lowercased for case-insensitive lookups. - `gui/SettingGui.java` вҖ” interactive 3-row chest GUI (`/fpp settings`). 7 categories: General, Body, Chat, Swap, Peak Hours, PvP, Pathfinding; up to 18 settings per page. Toggle items flip their boolean in-place on click; numeric/double items close the chest and prompt the player to type a value in chat (message cancelled вҖ” invisible to other players, 60-second timeout auto-cancels). After each change, calls `plugin.saveConfig()`, `Config.reload()`, and `applyLiveEffect(configKey)` вҖ” which mirrors `ReloadCommand`'s subsystem calls for instant live effect without a full `/fpp reload`. **Reset button** (slot 18): resets all settings on the current page to JAR defaults. Per-player session state stored in `sessions` map (keyed by UUID). Chat-input sessions tracked separately in `chatSessions`; `pendingChatInput` / `pendingRebuild` sets prevent false `InventoryCloseEvent` handling during transitions. `SettingGui` is a singleton instantiated in `onEnable`, passed to `SettingCommand`, and registered as a listener via `registerEvents(settingGui, this)`. **Permission:** `Perm.SETTINGS = "fpp.settings"` (default `op`). - `lang/Lang.java` вҖ” message rendering. Loads `language/en.yml`, supports named and positional placeholders. Use `Lang.get("key", "name", "value")` for an Adventure `Component` or `Lang.raw("key", ...)` for a raw string. Hot-reloadable via `/fpp reload`. - `permission/Perm.java` вҖ” central permission registry. All permission nodes declared as constants (e.g. `Perm.SPAWN`, `Perm.DELETE`, `Perm.USER_SPAWN`). Use `Perm.has()`, `Perm.hasOrOp()`, `Perm.resolveUserBotLimit()` helpers. Never hard-code permission strings in commands. - `database/DatabaseManager.java`, `database/BotRecord.java` вҖ” session history and analytics. Supports SQLite (default, WAL mode) and MySQL. Writes are serialised on a background thread; positions are batch-flushed on a configurable interval. Controlled by `Config.databaseEnabled()` and `Config.mysqlEnabled()`. **Schema version 8.** Tables: `fpp_bot_sessions` (session history), `fpp_active_bots` (restart persistence source of truth), `fpp_sleeping_bots` (peak-hours crash-safe sleeping-bot queue вҖ” `sleep_order`, `bot_name`, `world_name`, `pos_x/y/z`, `pos_yaw/pitch`, `server_id`; populated whenever the peak-hours sleep queue changes, cleared on `wakeAll()`), `fpp_bot_identities` (stable nameвҶ’UUID registry вҖ” `bot_name`, `server_id`, `bot_uuid`, `created_at`; PRIMARY KEY `(bot_name, server_id)`; backfilled from earliest session history on schema upgrade), `fpp_meta` (schema version). API: `saveSleepingBots(List<SleepingBotRow>)`, `loadSleepingBots()`, `clearSleepingBots()`, `lookupBotUuid(botName, serverId)` (synchronous read вҶ’ `UUID` or `null`), `registerBotUuid(botName, uuid, serverId)` (enqueued write вҖ” INSERT OR IGNORE so first UUID always wins). - `util/TabListManager.java`, `util/UpdateChecker.java` вҖ” tab header/footer management and async update checker initialized during startup.
3) Key patterns & concrete examples (copy-ready pointers) - Migration runs before config init: `ConfigMigrator.migrateIfNeeded(this)` in `onEnable` (see `FakePlayerPlugin.java`). - Config usage: call `Config.init(this)` then use accessors like `Config.fakeChatEnabled()` in code. Tab-list visibility accessor: `Config.tabListEnabled()` (key `tab-list.enabled`, default `true`) вҖ” when `false`, bots are never added to any player's tab list; the 20-tick display-name refresh loop, `syncToPlayer`, and the respawn re-add in `FakePlayerEntityListener` all check this flag before sending any `sendTabListAdd` packets. Toggling via `/fpp reload` calls `FakePlayerManager.applyTabListConfig()` which immediately removes or re-adds all active bots' tab entries for all online players. **Key accessors:** `Config.fakeChatEnabled()` (`fake-chat.enabled`, default `false`); `Config.fakeChatRequirePlayer()` (`fake-chat.require-player-online`, default `true`); `Config.fakeChatMessages()` (messages from `bot-messages.yml`). Local bots with a body use `player.chat(message)` so the server's real chat pipeline handles formatting. Bodyless bots and remote proxy bots use `Config.fakeChatRemoteFormat()` (`fake-chat.remote-format`, default `"<yellow>{name}<dark_gray>: <white>{message}"`) вҖ” supports `{name}` and `{message}` placeholders. `Config.bodyPushable()` (`body.pushable`, default `true`); `Config.bodyDamageable()` (`body.damageable`, default `true`); `Config.bodyPickUpItems()` (`body.pick-up-items`, default `false` вҖ” bots do not pick up items); `Config.bodyPickUpXp()` (`body.pick-up-xp`, default `true` вҖ” bots pick up XP orbs; set `false` to prevent all orb pickup; also honoured by the `XpCommand` post-collection cooldown via `BotXpPickupListener`); `Config.warningsNotifyAdmins()` (`messages.notify-admins-on-join`, default `true`) вҖ” send compatibility warning to online admins on startup. **Swap system config (v1.5.17):** `Config.swapMinOnline()` (`swap.min-online`, default `0` вҖ” minimum bots that must stay online; swap skips if removing one would drop below this floor); `Config.swapRetryRejoin()` (`swap.retry-rejoin`, default `true` вҖ” auto-retry failed rejoins); `Config.swapRetryDelay()` (`swap.retry-delay`, default `60` seconds вҖ” delay before retry). **Granular debug flags:** `Config.debugStartup()`, `Config.debugNms()`, `Config.debugPackets()`, `Config.debugLuckPerms()`, `Config.debugNetwork()`, `Config.debugConfigSync()`, `Config.debugSkin()`, `Config.debugDatabase()`, `Config.debugChat()`, `Config.debugSwap()` вҖ” each maps to `logging.debug.<category>` in config (also triggered by global `debug: true`). **Network/proxy accessors:** `Config.databaseMode()` (`database.mode`, `"LOCAL"` or `"NETWORK"`); `Config.isNetworkMode()` вҖ” shorthand for `databaseEnabled() && databaseMode()=="NETWORK"`; `Config.serverId()` (`database.server-id`, falls back to legacy `server.id`); `Config.configSyncMode()` (`config-sync.mode`, one of `DISABLED` / `MANUAL` / `AUTO_PULL` / `AUTO_PUSH`). Server-specific keys (`database.server-id`, `database.mysql.*`, `debug`) are never pushed/pulled by `ConfigSyncManager`. **Performance config (v1.5.17):** `Config.positionSyncDistance()` (`performance.position-sync-distance`, default `128.0` вҖ” max distance for position sync packets; `0` = unlimited). Note: `Config.tabListNameFormat()` and the `bot-name.tab-list-format` config key were **removed** in config v38 вҖ” LP manages prefix/suffix natively for NMS ServerPlayer entities and the display name packet carries only the bot name itself. - Language messages: always use `Lang.get("key")` (returns Adventure `Component`) or `Lang.raw("key")` (returns `String`) вҖ” never hard-code user-facing text. Named placeholders: `Lang.get("freeze-frozen", "name", fp.getDisplayName())`. All keys live in `src/main/resources/language/en.yml`. - Permission checks: use `Perm.has(sender, Perm.SPAWN)` or `Perm.hasOrOp(sender, Perm.OP)` вҖ” never hard-code node strings. **All permissions declared in `Perm.java` as constants AND in `plugin.yml` for LuckPerms auto-discovery.** Per-user bot limits resolved via `Perm.resolveUserBotLimit(sender)` (scans `fpp.spawn.limit.1` вҖҰ `fpp.spawn.limit.100`; returns -1 if none set, caller falls back to `Config.userBotLimit()`). **Permission structure:** `fpp.op` grants all commands (admin wildcard, default: op); `fpp.use` grants user-tier commands: spawn (with 1-bot limit), tph, xp, info-user (default: true вҖ” all players). Bot limits assigned via `fpp.spawn.limit.<num>` nodes (1-100); `fpp.spawn.limit.1` is included in `fpp.use` by default. New command permission constants: `Perm.INVENTORY = "fpp.inventory"`, `Perm.MOVE = "fpp.move"`, `Perm.USER_XP = "fpp.xp"` (user-tier, included in `fpp.use`), `Perm.USER_INFO = "fpp.info.user"`, `Perm.USER_TPH = "fpp.tph"`, `Perm.MIGRATE = "fpp.migrate"`. When adding new commands: (1) add constant to `Perm.java`, (2) add full entry to `plugin.yml` with description and default, (3) add to `fpp.op` children list in `plugin.yml`, (4) use constant in command's `getPermission()` and `canUse()` methods. **LuckPerms tab-completion depends on `plugin.yml` declarations** вҖ” missing entries won't appear in `/lp permission set fpp.<tab>`. - Command registration: plugin registers commands via `commandManager.register(new SpawnCommand(...))`; `HelpCommand` is auto-registered inside `CommandManager`, and `/fpp help` reads live from the registered command list, so new sub-commands need no separate help-menu wiring. The plugin then calls `getCommand("fpp").setExecutor(commandManager)` (see `onEnable`). - Listener registration: `getServer().getPluginManager().registerEvents(new PlayerJoinListener(this, fakePlayerManager), this)` вҖ” listeners receive plugin + manager references. - Persistence lifecycle: restore on enable via `botPersistence.purgeOrphanedBodiesAndRestore(fakePlayerManager)` (purges orphaned entities then calls `restore()` 5 ticks later) and save on disable via `botPersistence.save(fakePlayerManager.getActivePlayers())`. `BotPersistence.saveAsync()` also available for non-shutdown saves (snapshots locations on main thread, writes async). When DB is enabled, the `fpp_active_bots` table is the **primary** restore source (`DatabaseManager.getActiveBots()`); `data/active-bots.yml` is the fallback for no-DB or empty-table cases. - **Performance optimization (v1.5.17):** `FakePlayerManager.getByName()` is now O(1) via secondary `nameIndex` map (`ConcurrentHashMap<String, FakePlayer>`, lowercased keys) вҖ” was O(n) linear scan. The index is maintained at all add/remove sites. Additionally, position sync packets are distance-culled via `Config.positionSyncDistance()` (`performance.position-sync-distance`, default `128.0` blocks, `0` = unlimited) вҖ” only players within range receive per-tick position updates, reducing packet overhead on large servers. - Metrics & soft-deps: metrics are initialized with `FppMetrics` when `Config.metricsEnabled()`; PlaceholderAPI expansion registered conditionally in `onEnable`. - Compatibility checks: `CompatibilityChecker.check()` (in `util/CompatibilityChecker.java`) **always returns a non-restricted result** вҖ” the old Paper-detection and minimum MC version gate has been removed. `check()` is **not called in `onEnable`**; `compatibilityRestricted` and `compatibilityWarningMessage` fields have been removed from `FakePlayerPlugin`. The class retains two general-purpose utilities: `CompatibilityChecker.extractMcVersion()` (returns detected MC version string) and `CompatibilityChecker.isVersionAtLeast(version, required)` (component-wise numeric comparison). `buildWarningComponent(result)` always returns `null`. **Version gate (separate from `check()`):** `FakePlayerPlugin` directly calls `CompatibilityChecker.isVersionAtLeast(detectedMcVersion, "1.21.12")` in `onEnable` to set `versionUnsupported = true` when the server runs MC 1.21.12+. New fields: `versionUnsupported` (boolean) and `detectedMcVersion` (String); new accessors: `plugin.isVersionUnsupported()` and `plugin.getDetectedMcVersion()`. When `versionUnsupported = true`: `CommandManager` blocks all sub-commands (bare `/fpp` still shows plugin info), `PlayerJoinListener` sends the `"version-unsupported-admin"` Lang warning to operators on every join, and a prominent banner is printed to the console at startup. FPP is tested up to MC 1.21.11; any newer version triggers this gate. - Bot pools: `BotNameConfig.init(this)` and `BotMessageConfig.init(this)` are called during startup вҖ” the plugin loads `bot-names.yml` and `bot-messages.yml` early. - Metrics / FastStats packaging: `FppMetrics` is initialized before the startup banner to reflect metrics status. FastStats jars are bundled under `src/main/resources/faststats/` (the POM treats them as binary resources, not shaded). Note: building requires JDK 21 (see `pom.xml` <java.version>21). - Dependencies: **PacketEvents 2.x is required** вҖ” FPP uses it for tab-list packet integration. Declared in `plugin.yml` as a soft-depend: `PlaceholderAPI` and `LuckPerms` (both optional, auto-detected). - Entity stack: each bot is a single NMS ServerPlayer entity (created via `NmsPlayerSpawner`) with full player functionality including chat, tab-list presence, and realistic movement. Use PDC keys for identification during event handling. - Database gate: check `Config.databaseEnabled()` before any `DatabaseManager` call; `databaseManager` may be `null` at runtime (disabled or failed to init). `databaseManager.recordAllShutdown()` must be called in `onDisable` before `databaseManager.close()`. - Text formatting: `TextUtil.colorize(string)` parses MiniMessage tags and legacy `&`/`В§` codes into an Adventure `Component`. `TextUtil.format(string)` additionally applies small-caps Unicode conversion. **Supports all modern color formats:** MiniMessage tags (`<rainbow>`, `<gradient:#FF0000:#0000FF>`), hex colors (`<#9782ff>`), LuckPerms gradient shorthand (`{#fffff>}text{#00000<}`), and mixed formats (`&7[<#9782ff>Phantom</#9782ff>&7]`). - LuckPerms integration: `LuckPermsHelper` reads resolved prefix/suffix directly from LP's in-memory `CachedMetaData` вҖ” bots are real NMS `ServerPlayer` entities, LP handles them natively. Core helpers: `LuckPermsHelper.isAvailable()`, `LuckPermsHelper.groupExists(groupName)`, `LuckPermsHelper.getAllGroupNames()`, `LuckPermsHelper.buildGroupSummary()`. Runtime group assignment: `LuckPermsHelper.setPlayerGroup(uuid, groupName)` (works online or offline) or `LuckPermsHelper.applyGroupToOnlineUser(uuid, groupName)` (prefers the live `User` instance so `UserDataRecalculateEvent` fires immediately вҖ” preferred when bot is already spawned). Prefix/suffix reads: `LuckPermsHelper.getResolvedPrefix(uuid)` / `LuckPermsHelper.getResolvedSuffix(uuid)` (synchronous, requires bot to be online). Async group read from storage: `LuckPermsHelper.getStoredPrimaryGroup(uuid)`. Online-only read: `LuckPermsHelper.getPrimaryGroup(uuid)`. Force LP metadata refresh: `LuckPermsHelper.refreshUserCache(uuid)`. `LuckPermsHelper.invalidateCache()` is `@Deprecated` (no-op вҖ” LP handles caching natively). **Event subscription:** `LuckPermsHelper.subscribeLpEvents(plugin, manager)` subscribes to `UserDataRecalculateEvent` so bot display names auto-update whenever a group changes (LP command, API, etc.); called directly in `onEnable` (not deferred). `LuckPermsHelper.unsubscribeLpEvents()` is called in `onDisable` to prevent memory leaks. **LP group assignment:** `Config.luckpermsDefaultGroup()` (`luckperms.default-group`, default `""`) вҖ” optional LP group assigned at spawn; blank = `"default"`. Use `/fpp lpinfo [bot-name]` for in-game diagnostics. Check LP availability via `LuckPermsHelper.isAvailable()` before any API call. **LP injection flow:** `finishSpawn` calls `LuckPermsHelper.ensureGroupBeforeSpawn(botUuid, cfgGroup)` **async before the NMS body spawns** вҖ” writes the group to LP storage so `PlayerJoinEvent` listeners (TAB plugin, etc.) see the correct group immediately. If the bot already has a non-default group in storage (prior `/fpp rank`), that group is preserved unless `luckperms.default-group` overrides it. Once LP completes, `spawnBodyAndFinish(fp, spawnLoc)` runs on the main thread. After the body spawns, `LuckPermsHelper.applyGroupToOnlineUser(uuid, group)` is called 5 ticks later; after that completes, `refreshLpDisplayName(fp)` is called 2 ticks later to update the tab-list display name. `FakePlayer.rawDisplayName` is the stable `{bot_name}` base for rebuilds. Call `FakePlayerManager.refreshLpDisplayName(fp)` to manually rebuild display name + resend tab-list update packets. **Scoreboard team override:** All bots are placed in the `~fpp` scoreboard team (managed by `BotTabTeam`) which sorts AFTER all other teams, ensuring bots always appear below real players regardless of LP group weight. Note: `LpData` record, `getBotLpData()`, `buildPacketProfileName()`, `invalidateGroup()`, `refreshRankList()`, and the packet-prefix-char weight-rank ordering system have all been removed. **ClassLoader guard:** `FakePlayerPlugin` caches a `luckPermsAvailable` boolean (set once in `onEnable()` via `Bukkit.getPluginManager().getPlugin("LuckPerms") != null`) accessible via `plugin.isLuckPermsAvailable()`. **Never reference `LuckPermsHelper` or any LP API class unless this returns `true`** вҖ” Paper's `PluginClassLoader` eagerly resolves LP API class references during bytecode verification, so even calling `LuckPermsHelper.isAvailable()` on a server without LP installed throws `NoClassDefFoundError`. The pattern everywhere LP is needed: `if (plugin.isLuckPermsAvailable()) { LuckPermsHelper.doSomething(...); }`. - WorldGuard integration: `WorldGuardHelper` (in `util/WorldGuardHelper.java`) provides PvP region protection for bots. Key methods: `WorldGuardHelper.isPvpAllowed(location)` вҖ” queries active WG region flags; returns `true` when no region explicitly sets PVP to DENY (fail-open: unprotected areas always allow PvP, only explicit DENY blocks damage); `WorldGuardHelper.findSafeLocation(world)` вҖ” returns the world spawn if PvP is allowed there, otherwise `null`. Used by `FakePlayerEntityListener` at `LOWEST` priority to cancel player-sourced PvP damage to bots inside no-PvP regions. **ClassLoader guard:** `FakePlayerPlugin` caches `worldGuardAvailable` (set once via `Bukkit.getPluginManager().getPlugin("WorldGuard") != null`) accessible via `plugin.isWorldGuardAvailable()`. **Never reference `WorldGuardHelper` unless this returns `true`** вҖ” same NoClassDefFoundError risk as LuckPerms. Pattern: `if (plugin.isWorldGuardAvailable()) { WorldGuardHelper.doSomething(...); }`. `WorldGuard` is declared in `plugin.yml` as a soft-depend. - Listener registration: `getServer().getPluginManager().registerEvents(new PlayerJoinListener(this, fakePlayerManager), this)` вҖ” listeners receive plugin + manager references. `FakePlayerKickListener` (guards all bots from automated kicks like anti-idle/anti-AFK) is always registered; all other listeners (`FakePlayerEntityListener`, `BotCollisionListener`, etc.) are also always registered вҖ” the old `compatibilityRestricted` skip is gone. - `onDisable` sequence: `chunkLoader.releaseAll()` вҶ’ `botChatAI.cancelAll()` вҶ’ **`peakHoursManager.shutdown()`** (wakes all sleeping bots so they appear in `activePlayers`) вҶ’ `botSwapAI.cancelAll()` вҶ’ **save persistence** (sleeping bots are now active and captured) вҶ’ `LuckPermsHelper.unsubscribeLpEvents()` вҶ’ `fakePlayerManager.removeAllSync()` (also calls `pvpAI.cancelAll()` internally) вҶ’ `tabListManager.shutdown()` вҶ’ `botTabTeam.destroy()` вҶ’ `databaseManager.recordAllShutdown()` + `close()` вҶ’ `fppMetrics.shutdown()` вҶ’ unregister VelocityChannel messaging channels. **Critical ordering:** `peakHoursManager.shutdown()` MUST run before `botPersistence.save()` so sleeping bots are woken and included in the persistence file. - Update notifications to late-joining admins: stored as a `Component` on `FakePlayerPlugin.getUpdateNotification()` / `setUpdateNotification()`; `PlayerJoinListener` delivers it to players with `Perm.ALL`. - YAML file sync: `YamlFileSyncer.syncMissingKeys(plugin, diskRelPath, jarResourcePath)` вҖ” non-destructive; adds keys present in the JAR but absent from disk, never overwrites user values. Returns a `SyncResult` record (`fileName`, `keysAdded`, `hasChanges()`, `count()`). Called automatically inside `Lang.reload()`, `BotNameConfig.reload()`, and `BotMessageConfig.reload()` on every startup and `/fpp reload`. Force-run from the command line with `/fpp migrate lang`, `/fpp migrate names`, or `/fpp migrate messages`. When adding new keys to `en.yml`, `bot-names.yml`, or `bot-messages.yml`, they will be automatically synced to existing user files on next reload вҖ” no manual migration step needed. - Spawn cooldown: `Config.spawnCooldown()` (key `spawn-cooldown`, default `0` = disabled). Applies to both admin and user `/fpp spawn`. Players with `Perm.BYPASS_COOLDOWN` (`fpp.bypass.cooldown`) are exempt. Cooldown state lives in `FakePlayerManager.spawnCooldowns` (per-UUID map). Helpers: `manager.isOnCooldown(uuid)`, `manager.getRemainingCooldown(uuid)` (seconds), `manager.recordSpawnCooldown(uuid)`. - Backup variants: `BackupManager.createFullBackup(plugin, reason)` вҖ” backs up all plugin files including SQLite; used by `ConfigMigrator` before migrations. `BackupManager.createConfigFilesBackup(plugin, reason)` вҖ” backs up YAML files only (`config.yml`, `bot-names.yml`, `bot-messages.yml`, `language/`); used before lightweight sync operations (e.g. `/fpp migrate lang`). Both methods prune to the most recent `MAX_BACKUPS` (10) automatically. - Additional `Perm` helpers: `Perm.missing(sender, node)` returns `true` when the sender lacks the node (negation of `has()`); `Perm.hasAny(sender, node1, node2, ...)` returns `true` when the sender has at least one of the listed nodes. - Physical-body API: `FakePlayerManager.physicalBodiesEnabled()` вҖ” returns `Config.spawnBody()`. (The old `&& !plugin.isCompatibilityRestricted()` check has been removed.) Use this instead of checking `Config.spawnBody()` inline when deciding whether NMS ServerPlayer bodies are active. - Display-name pipeline: `FakePlayerManager.finalizeDisplayName(rawName, botName)` вҖ” private helper that expands PAPI `%placeholders%` via `PlaceholderAPI.setPlaceholders(null, ...)` (null player = server-wide), then calls `sanitizeDisplayName()`. **Note:** the `{prefix}/{bot_name}/{suffix}` tab-list format substitution (previously driven by `bot-name.tab-list-format`) was removed in config v38 вҖ” LP manages prefix/suffix natively for real NMS ServerPlayer entities; the display name packet carries only the bot name. Called at every display-name build site: `spawnUserBot`, `spawn`, `spawnRestored`, `validateUserBotNames`. Public entry point for post-LP-assignment rebuilds: `FakePlayerManager.refreshLpDisplayName(fp)` (also has a private retry variant `refreshLpDisplayNameWithRetry` for LP metadata race conditions). - Body damageable/pushable live-reload: `FakePlayerManager.applyBodyConfig()` вҖ” called on `/fpp reload`; handles damage/pushable configuration for all active NMS ServerPlayer entities. `BotCollisionListener` also guards all three push paths (`onEntityDamageByEntity`, `onPlayerMove`, `tickBotSeparation`) with `if (!Config.bodyPushable()) return;`. - Swim AI: `Config.swimAiEnabled()` (`swim-ai.enabled`, default `true`). Each tick the main loop calls `NmsPlayerSpawner.setJumping(bot, bot.isInWater() || bot.isInLava())` so bots automatically swim upward when submerged, mimicking a player holding spacebar. Set `swim-ai.enabled: false` to let bots sink/drown instead. - Pathfinding config (used by `/fpp move`): `Config.pathfindingParkour()` (`pathfinding.parkour`, default `false`); `Config.pathfindingBreakBlocks()` (`pathfinding.break-blocks`, default `false`); `Config.pathfindingPlaceBlocks()` (`pathfinding.place-blocks`, default `false`); `Config.pathfindingPlaceMaterial()` (`pathfinding.place-material`, default `"DIRT"` вҖ” material placed when bridging gaps; falls back to `DIRT` if the value is not a valid solid block). These correspond to `BotPathfinder.PathOptions` fields and are re-read on every path recalculation. - PVP AI subsystem: `BotPvpAI` drives all movement and combat for `BotType.PVP` bots. Initialized in `FakePlayerManager` constructor; accessed via `manager.getPvpAI()`. The main tick loop calls `pvpAI.tickBot(fp, bot, online)` each game tick for PVP bots only (head-AI is skipped for PVP bots вҖ” `BotPvpAI` handles rotation internally). Key config: `Config.pvpAiDifficulty()` (`pvp-ai.difficulty`, default `"medium"`, values: `easy`/`medium`/`hard`/`tier1`/`hacker`); `Config.pvpAiDefensiveMode()` (`pvp-ai.defensive-mode`, default `true` вҖ” bots only attack back when hit); `Config.pvpAiDetectRange()` (`pvp-ai.detect-range`, default `32.0`). When a player damages a PVP bot, `FakePlayerEntityListener` calls `manager.getPvpAI().onBotAttacked(botUuid, attackerUuid)` to register the attacker for defensive retaliation. Combat mode (`BotCombatMode.CRYSTAL`/`SWORD`/`FIST`) is auto-detected from the bot's inventory every 2 seconds. Difficulty controls attack range, crit/s-tap consistency, and early-swing window. **вҡ PVP bots are currently restricted to the internal developer UUID вҖ” not yet available for general spawning.** - BotChatAI expanded features: Each bot is assigned a random activity tier at first use вҖ” quiet (15%), passive (25%), normal (30%), active (18%), chatty (12%) вҖ” which scales the chat interval multiplier. Override per-bot via `FakePlayer.setChatTier(String)` or via `/fpp chat <bot> tier <tier>`; reset to random with `tier reset`. Silence a bot permanently (`/fpp chat <bot> off` or `fp.setChatEnabled(false)`) or temporarily (`/fpp chat <bot> mute [seconds]` вҖ” `BotChatAI.timedMute(uuid, seconds)`). Inspect state with `/fpp chat <bot> info` (shows enabled, tier, multiplier). Bulk operations: `/fpp chat all <on|off|tier <tier>|mute [seconds]>`. **Chat realism config (v39вҶ’v40):** `Config.fakeChatTypingDelay()` (`fake-chat.typing-delay`, default `true`), `Config.fakeChatBurstChance()` (`fake-chat.burst-chance`, default `0.12`), `Config.fakeChatBurstDelayMin()/Max()` (`fake-chat.burst-delay.min/max`, default `2/5`), `Config.fakeChatReplyToMentions()` (`fake-chat.reply-to-mentions`, default `true`), `Config.fakeChatMentionReplyChance()` (`fake-chat.mention-reply-chance`, default `0.65`), `Config.fakeChatReplyDelayMin()/Max()` (`fake-chat.reply-delay.min/max`, default `2/8`), `Config.fakeChatStaggerInterval()` (`fake-chat.stagger-interval`, default `3`), `Config.fakeChatActivityVariation()` (`fake-chat.activity-variation`, default `true`), `Config.fakeChatHistorySize()` (`fake-chat.history-size`, default `5`). Event-triggered chat config: `fake-chat.event-triggers.enabled`, `.on-player-join.*`, `.on-death.*`, `.on-player-leave.*`. Keyword reactions: `fake-chat.keyword-reactions`. **`BotMessageConfig` message pools** (all in `bot-messages.yml`, auto-synced via `YamlFileSyncer`): `messages` (base chat pool), `replies` (mention-reply pool вҖ” `Config.chatReplyMessages()`), `burst-followups` (burst follow-up pool вҖ” `Config.chatBurstMessages()`), `join-reactions` / `death-reactions` / `leave-reactions` / `keyword-reactions.<key>` (event-triggered pools вҖ” `Config.chatJoinReactionMessages()`, `chatDeathReactionMessages()`, `chatLeaveReactionMessages()`, `chatKeywordReactionMessages(key)`). Accessed via `FakePlayerPlugin.getBotChatAI()` or `FakePlayerManager.getBotChatAI()`. - Peak-hours subsystem: `PeakHoursManager` controls time-window-based bot pool scheduling. Requires `swap.enabled: true`. **Config accessors:** `Config.peakHoursEnabled()` (`peak-hours.enabled`, default `false`); `Config.peakHoursTimezone()` (`peak-hours.timezone`, default `"UTC"`); `Config.peakHoursStaggerSeconds()` (`peak-hours.stagger-seconds`, default `30`); `Config.peakHoursSchedule()` (`peak-hours.schedule` вҖ” list of `{start, end, fraction}` maps); `Config.peakHoursDayOverrides()` (`peak-hours.day-overrides` вҖ” per-`DayOfWeek` schedule overrides, keyed by uppercase `DayOfWeek` name); `Config.peakHoursMinOnline()` (`peak-hours.min-online`, default `0` = no floor); `Config.peakHoursNotifyTransitions()` (`peak-hours.notify-transitions`, default `false` вҖ” broadcasts window changes to players with `Perm.PEAKS`). **Permission:** `Perm.PEAKS = "fpp.peaks"`. **Command:** `/fpp peaks [on|off|status|next|force|list|wake [name]|sleep <name>]` вҖ” toggle, inspect, or manually override the sleeping pool. **Hot-reload:** `PeakHoursManager.reload()` is called by `ReloadCommand` (step 3b) вҖ” wakes all sleeping bots, resets transition state, then restarts the tick if still enabled. **`onDisable` ordering:** `peakHoursManager.shutdown()` wakes all sleeping bots BEFORE `botPersistence.save()` so the full pool is captured. **Crash recovery:** `peakHoursManager.restoreSleepingBotsFromDatabase(db)` is called after `botPersistence.purgeOrphanedBodiesAndRestore()` to repopulate the sleeping-bot deque from `fpp_sleeping_bots`; only runs when `databaseManager != null && Config.persistOnRestart()`. - `FakePlayer.getLiveLocation()` вҖ” returns the most accurate current location: the live NMS Player body position when valid, otherwise the last recorded `spawnLocation`. Used by the DB flush loop in `FakePlayerManager` and anywhere a fresh location is needed without relying on stored state. - `FakePlayerManager.restorationInProgress` вҖ” boolean flag (with `isRestorationInProgress()` / `setRestorationInProgress()`) that is `true` during bot persistence restore and cleared after LP prefix refresh completes. Check this before triggering actions that should not run while bots are being restored.
4) Common tasks & minimal steps (how to implement typical changes) - Add a new command: - Create `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/command/MyCommand.java` implementing the command interface used by `CommandManager`. - Register it in `FakePlayerPlugin.onEnable()` with `commandManager.register(new MyCommand(...))`. - No manual help update is needed вҖ” `HelpCommand` reads live from `CommandManager`. - Ensure `plugin.yml` contains the `fpp` command or sub-command docs (command parsing done by `CommandManager`). - Add a config option: - Add default to `src/main/resources/config.yml`. - If needed, update `Config` accessor and `ConfigMigrator` to preserve old configs. - New options should be validated in `util/ConfigValidator.java` if they can be misconfigured. - Add a language message: - Add the key and default text to `src/main/resources/language/en.yml`. - Access it in code with `Lang.get("your-key")` or `Lang.raw("your-key", "placeholder", "value")`. - The file is hot-reloadable вҖ” `/fpp reload` picks up changes without restart. - Add a permission node: - Declare a new `public static final String` constant in `permission/Perm.java` (e.g. `public static final String MY_FEATURE = "fpp.myfeature";`). - Add a full entry under `permissions:` in `plugin.yml` with description and default value (e.g. `op`, `true`, or `false`). - Add the node to the `fpp.*` wildcard's `children:` list in `plugin.yml` (so admins inherit it automatically). - Use the constant in your command's `getPermission()` and `canUse()` methods: `Perm.has(sender, Perm.MY_FEATURE)`. - **Critical:** LuckPerms reads `plugin.yml` at startup for tab-completion вҖ” permissions missing from `plugin.yml` won't appear in `/lp permission set fpp.<tab>`. - Test: restart server, run `/lp user <name> permission set fpp.` and verify your new permission appears in tab-completion.
- If changing skin behaviour or adding skins: ensure `SkinRepository`/`SkinFetcher` are respected вҖ” add skin files to `src/main/resources/skins/` for defaults or `plugins/FakePlayerPlugin/skins/` at runtime and call `/fpp reload` to refresh. The startup code also drops a `skins/README.txt` inside the plugin data folder on first run. **Note (v1.5.4):** `skin.guaranteed-skin` default changed from `true` вҶ’ `false`; bots with non-Mojang names now use Steve/Alex skins by default. `skin.fallback-pool` (`Config.skinFallbackPool()`) and `skin.fallback-name` (`Config.skinFallbackName()`) still exist as auto-mode fallbacks for guaranteed-skin resolution.
5) Build / run / verify (Windows PowerShell) Build, obfuscate, AND auto-deploy to `%USERPROFILE%\Desktop\dmc\plugins\fpp.jar` in one command: ```powershell mvn -DskipTests clean package ``` The `maven-antrun-plugin` in `pom.xml` automatically copies `target/fpp-*-obfuscated.jar` to the deploy folder after ProGuard finishes. To deploy to a different path override the property: ```powershell mvn -DskipTests clean package "-Ddeploy.dir=C:\path\to\server\plugins" ``` Note: requires JDK 21+ to compile (`pom.xml` `<java.version>21`). FastStats jars are bundled as binary resources in `src/main/resources/faststats/` (not shaded).
**IntelliJ build tool ("Build Artifacts"):** IntelliJ's native artifact builder bypasses Maven and ProGuard. Treat `target/fpp-*-obfuscated.jar` as the source artifact and use `pom.xml` as the source of truth for the current version; some repo docs/scripts still mention `1.4.22`. Workflow: 1. Run the **"Build Plugin (Obfuscated)"** Maven run configuration (or `mvn clean package`) вҖ” this runs ProGuard and auto-deploys 2. Optionally run **Build вҖә Build Artifacts** to re-pack the already-obfuscated JAR to the output path without recompiling - вҡ пёҸ "Build Artifacts" alone (without Maven running first) will fail if `target/fpp-*-obfuscated.jar` does not exist
Double-click `BUILD.bat` in the project root as an alternative to Maven in terminal.
For the optional Node/Vercel sidecar (`frontend/`), use the root `package.json` scripts: `npm install`, then `npm start` (local Express server via `frontend/index.js`) or `npm run dev`.
Start server (attach debugger): ```powershell java -agentlib:jdwp=transport=dt_socket,server=y,suspend=n,address=*:5005 -Xms512M -Xmx2G -jar .\paper-1.21.11.jar nogui ``` Tail logs: ```powershell Get-Content .\logs\latest.log -Wait ``` In-server quick checks: `/fpp list`, `/fpp spawn`, `/fpp reload` and watch console for startup banner messages printed by `FppLogger`.
6) Debugging tips - To reproduce startup ordering bugs, inspect `onEnable()` in `FakePlayerPlugin.java` вҖ” migration вҶ’ config вҶ’ Lang вҶ’ **version gate** (`CompatibilityChecker.isVersionAtLeast(detectedMcVersion, "1.21.12")` вҖ” sets `versionUnsupported`; runs between Lang and BotNameConfig) вҶ’ BotNameConfig/BotMessageConfig вҶ’ ensureDataDirectories вҶ’ SkinRepository.init вҶ’ database вҶ’ **BotIdentityCache** вҶ’ remote bot cache (NETWORK mode: pre-populate from DB) вҶ’ ConfigSyncManager (NETWORK mode only) вҶ’ FakePlayerManager+subsystems вҶ’ commands (**SettingGui instantiated here; `SettingCommand` registered; `settingGui` registered as listener immediately after**) вҶ’ listeners вҶ’ **BotChatAI + BotSwapAI + PeakHoursManager init** (PeakHoursManager started if `peak-hours.enabled && swap.enabled`) вҶ’ VelocityChannel (plugin messaging) вҶ’ periodic health check (6000 ticks) вҶ’ ConfigValidator вҶ’ TabListManager вҶ’ BotTabTeam вҶ’ PlaceholderAPI вҶ’ LuckPerms (`subscribeLpEvents` вҖ” direct, not deferred) вҶ’ **WorldGuard** (sets `worldGuardAvailable`) вҶ’ UpdateChecker (purely async, never blocks startup) вҶ’ Metrics вҶ’ startup banner вҶ’ persistence restore вҶ’ **PeakHoursManager crash recovery** (`restoreSleepingBotsFromDatabase` вҖ” only when DB enabled and `persist-on-restart: true`). Note: `CompatibilityChecker.check()` is no longer called at startup вҖ” all features are enabled unconditionally. - To debug config migrations, read `util/ConfigMigrator.java` and check backups under `plugins/FakePlayerPlugin/backups/` created by `BackupManager`. - For packet-level issues (spawn / entity problems) inspect `fakeplayer/PacketHelper.java` and `fakeplayer/FakePlayerManager.java`. - For database issues: SQLite file is at `plugins/FakePlayerPlugin/data/fpp.db`; run `/fpp migrate status` to check schema version, YAML sync health, and backup count. Full `/fpp migrate` subcommands: `backup` (create manual backup), `backups` (list stored backups), `status` (config version + file sync health + DB stats), `config` (re-run config migration chain), `lang` / `names` / `messages` (force-sync the respective YAML file from JAR), `db merge [file]` / `db export` / `db tomysql`. Set `database.enabled: false` in config to disable all DB I/O. - Tab list header/footer and update checker: `TabListManager.start()` and `UpdateChecker.check(this)` are run during `onEnable()` вҖ” useful when diagnosing missing tab headers or unexpected update-checker output.
7) Integration & soft-dependencies - PlaceholderAPI: `util.FppPlaceholderExpansion` registered if plugin present. **Full placeholder list (29+ total)** вҖ” server-wide: `%fpp_count%` (bot count вҖ” local + remote in NETWORK mode), `%fpp_local_count%` (this server's bots only), `%fpp_network_count%` (remote servers' bots only), `%fpp_max%` (global cap, `вҲһ` if 0), `%fpp_real%` (non-bot players online), `%fpp_total%` (real + bots, network-wide), `%fpp_online%` (alias for `%fpp_total%`), `%fpp_frozen%`, `%fpp_names%` (comma-joined display names вҖ” local + remote in NETWORK mode), `%fpp_network_names%` (remote-server bot names only), `%fpp_chat%`, `%fpp_swap%`, `%fpp_skin%`, `%fpp_body%`, `%fpp_pushable%`, `%fpp_damageable%`, `%fpp_tab%`, `%fpp_max_health%`, `%fpp_persistence%`, `%fpp_version%`. Network/Proxy: `%fpp_network%` (on/off for NETWORK mode), `%fpp_server_id%`, `%fpp_spawn_cooldown%`. Per-world: `%fpp_count_<world>%`, `%fpp_real_<world>%`, `%fpp_total_<world>%`. Player-relative: `%fpp_user_count%` (bots owned by that player), `%fpp_user_max%` (personal limit), `%fpp_user_names%` (comma-joined names of that player's bots). `%fpp_real%` = `Bukkit.getOnlinePlayers().size() - localBots` вҖ” bots go through `placeNewPlayer()` so they appear in `getOnlinePlayers()`; FPP subtracts local bot count to get the true real-player count. `%fpp_total%` = `real + bots`. - LuckPerms: `LuckPermsHelper.subscribeLpEvents(plugin, manager)` is called directly in `onEnable` (not deferred) for auto-prefix/suffix refresh via `UserDataRecalculateEvent`. - WorldGuard: soft-depend declared in `plugin.yml`. `WorldGuardHelper.isPvpAllowed(location)` cancels player-sourced PvP damage to bots in no-PvP regions (handled in `FakePlayerEntityListener` at `LOWEST` priority). `worldGuardAvailable` is cached in `onEnable` via a pure Bukkit plugin check вҖ” **never call `WorldGuardHelper` directly without first checking `plugin.isWorldGuardAvailable()`** (same ClassLoader guard as LuckPerms). - Vercel / Node API: `UpdateChecker` falls back from Modrinth to `https://fake-player-plugin.vercel.app`; `frontend/api/check-update.js` and `frontend/api/status.js` intentionally emit Java-compatible keys such as `remoteVersion`, `version`, `version_number`, and `downloadUrl`. - Metrics: `util/FppMetrics` wraps FastStats and is conditional via `config.yml` flag (`Config.metricsEnabled()`).
8) Useful repo search queries (quick semantic/grep targets) - "onEnable(" вҖ” find startup flow - "Config.init(" вҖ” find config usage - "getCommand(\"fpp\")" вҖ” command wiring - "registerEvents(" вҖ” listener registration - "purgeOrphanedBodiesAndRestore(" вҖ” persistence restore - "spawnFakePlayer(" or "PacketHelper" вҖ” low-level spawn - "Lang.get(" вҖ” message rendering pattern - "Perm." вҖ” permission check usages - "FakePlayerBody.spawn" вҖ” entity spawning entry point - "implements FppCommand" вҖ” all sub-command implementations - "DatabaseManager" вҖ” database call sites - "YamlFileSyncer.syncMissingKeys" вҖ” YAML auto-sync call sites (Lang, BotNameConfig, BotMessageConfig) - "isOnCooldown" вҖ” spawn cooldown enforcement - "updateBotPrefix(" or "refreshLpDisplayName(" вҖ” single-bot LP prefix refresh (called by finishSpawn and RankCommand) - "implements FppCommand" or "RankCommand" вҖ” find rank/LP group assignment logic - "VelocityChannel" вҖ” proxy plugin-messaging send/receive - "RemoteBotCache" вҖ” remote bot tab-list entries from other servers - "isNetworkMode" вҖ” NETWORK mode guard sites - "ConfigSyncManager" вҖ” config sync push/pull - "BotPvpAI" or "pvpAI.tickBot" вҖ” PVP bot AI tick loop and state machine - "BotType.PVP" or "getBotType()" вҖ” PVP bot type checks and spawn guards - "InventoryCommand" or "openGui" or "invToBot" вҖ” bot inventory GUI implementation - "MoveCommand" or "startNavigation" or "BotPathfinder" вҖ” bot pathfinding / /fpp move - "requestNavJump" or "clearNavJump" or "navJumpHolding" вҖ” navigation jump coordination in FakePlayerManager - "XpCommand" or "isOnXpCooldown" or "BotXpPickupListener" вҖ” XP collection command and pickup gating - "setChatEnabled" or "setChatTier" or "timedMute" вҖ” per-bot chat control - "BotCombatMode" вҖ” crystal/sword/fist combat mode detection - "PeakHoursManager" or "peakHoursManager" вҖ” time-window bot pool scheduler - "putBotToSleepByName" or "wakeBotByName" вҖ” manual peak-hours sleep/wake - "restoreSleepingBotsFromDatabase" вҖ” peak-hours crash recovery on startup - "SettingGui" or "applyLiveEffect" вҖ” in-game settings GUI and live config-key effect application - "BotIdentityCache" or "resolveUuid" or "identityCache" вҖ” stable nameвҶ’UUID identity system (prevents bots from appearing as new players on re-spawn) - "lookupBotUuid" or "registerBotUuid" вҖ” DatabaseManager identity API called by BotIdentityCache - "bot-identities.yml" вҖ” YAML fallback for identity persistence when database is disabled - "broadcastBotDisplayNameUpdate" вҖ” VelocityChannel BOT_UPDATE subchannel for display name sync - "WorldGuardHelper" or "isWorldGuardAvailable" вҖ” WorldGuard PvP region protection integration - "isPvpAllowed" вҖ” WorldGuard PvP flag query in FakePlayerEntityListener - "versionUnsupported" or "isVersionUnsupported" вҖ” MC version gate (blocks all /fpp sub-commands on MC 1.21.12+) - "stampFirstPlayed" or "forceHasPlayedBefore" вҖ” PlayerJoinListener reflection helpers for management plugin compat
9) Where to read docs - `docs/` вҖ” internal build/setup/release notes (`BUILD_INSTRUCTIONS.md`, `INTELLIJ_BUILD_SETUP.md`, `OBFUSCATION_CORRECTED.md`, `PROJECT_ORGANIZATION.md`, `RELEASE-1.4.23.md`, `RELEASE-1.5.8.md`, `SKIN-SYSTEM-FIX.md`, `BOT-SYSTEM-IMPLEMENTATION-STATUS.md`, `BOT-SYSTEM-IMPROVEMENTS.md`, `FIX-AI-STATE-CLEANUP.md`); current plugin version is `1.5.17`, current `config-version` stamped by `ConfigMigrator.CURRENT_VERSION` is `51` вҖ” always use `pom.xml` as the source of truth because some repo docs/scripts still mention `1.4.22`. Workflow: - `wiki/` вҖ” developer-facing docs (Commands.md, Configuration.md, Getting-Started.md, Database.md, Language.md, Permissions.md, Skin-System.md, Swap-System.md, Bot-Behaviour.md, Bot-Messages.md, Bot-Names.md, Fake-Chat.md, FAQ.md, Migration.md, Home.md, Placeholders.md, Proxy-Support.md, Config-Sync.md, **Peak-Hours.md**) - `README.md` вҖ” general usage and release notes
Contact / notes - This file focuses on discoverable, actionable patterns only. For design rationale, see `wiki/Migration.md` and issues in the project tracker.

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# AGENTS.md вҖ” Guide for AI coding agents
Purpose: help automated agents quickly understand, modify, and verify the FakePlayerPlugin codebase.
1) Quick repo map - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/` вҖ” main code and entry points - `src/main/resources/` вҖ” default `config.yml`, `plugin.yml`, `bot-names.yml`, `bot-messages.yml` - `src/main/resources/language/en.yml` вҖ” all user-facing messages (loaded by `Lang.java`) - `pom.xml` вҖ” build + jar metadata (`target/fpp-*.jar` produced) - `frontend/`, root `package.json`, and `vercel.json` вҖ” separate Node/Vercel status + update API sidecar used by the pluginвҖҷs update-check fallback; not part of the Maven plugin jar build. - `wiki/` вҖ” feature docs (Commands.md, Configuration.md, Getting-Started.md, Database.md, Language.md, Permissions.md, Skin-System.md, Swap-System.md, Bot-Behaviour.md, Bot-Messages.md, Bot-Names.md, Fake-Chat.md, FAQ.md, Migration.md, Home.md, Proxy-Support.md, Config-Sync.md, Placeholders.md) - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/gui/` вҖ” in-game settings GUI (`SettingGui`): 3-row chest that lets admins toggle booleans and set numeric/double config values without editing `config.yml` directly. Opened via `/fpp settings`. Implements `Listener` and is registered in `onEnable` via `registerEvents(settingGui, this)`. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/util/` вҖ” helper subsystems and utilities (e.g. `FppMetrics`, `FppLogger`, `FppPlaceholderExpansion`, `TabListManager`, `BackupManager`, `UpdateChecker`, `ConfigValidator`, `DataMigrator`, `TextUtil`, `ConfigMigrator`, `LuckPermsHelper`, `CompatibilityChecker`, `YamlFileSyncer`, `BotTabTeam`, `WorldGuardHelper`). - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/listener/` вҖ” Bukkit event listeners (`PlayerJoinListener`, `FakePlayerEntityListener`, `FakePlayerKickListener`, `BotCollisionListener`, `PlayerWorldChangeListener`, `ServerListListener`, `BotCommandBlocker`, `BotSpawnProtectionListener`, `BotXpPickupListener`). Note: `LuckPermsUpdateListener` was removed вҖ” LP now detects NMS bots as real players automatically. `BotCommandBlocker` prevents bots from executing any commands. `BotSpawnProtectionListener` prevents lobby/spawn plugins from teleporting bots during their 5-tick spawn grace period. `BotXpPickupListener` gates XP orb pickup (`PlayerPickupExperienceEvent`) and programmatic XP grants (`PlayerExpChangeEvent`) for bots via `Config.bodyPickUpXp()` and the `XpCommand` post-collection cooldown. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/fakeplayer/` вҖ” bot domain objects and subsystems (e.g. `FakePlayer`, `FakePlayerBody`, `FakePlayerManager`, `BotPersistence`, `ChunkLoader`, `SkinRepository`, `SkinFetcher`, `SkinProfile`, `SkinProfileInjector`, `NmsHelper`, `NmsPlayerSpawner`, `NmsReflection`, `BotSwapAI`, `BotChatAI`, `BotBroadcast`, `PacketHelper`, `RemoteBotCache`, `RemoteBotEntry`, `BotType`, `BotCombatMode`, `BotPvpAI`, `PeakHoursManager`, `BotPathfinder`). **BotSwapAI rejoin lifecycle (v1.5.17 critical fix):** rejoin task is now registered AFTER `delete()` completes so `cancel(uuid)` never silently kills the rejoin timer вҖ” bots actually come back. New config: `swap.min-online` (floor that prevents swaps if removing one would drop below), `swap.retry-rejoin` (auto-retry failed rejoins, default `true`), `swap.retry-delay` (seconds before retry, default `60`). Bot identification on rejoin: same-name rejoins use `getByName()` (O(1) `nameIndex` lookup); random-name rejoins use UUID diff. Sub-package `fakeplayer/network/` contains the fake Netty layer: `FakeChannel` (discard channel used by `FakeConnection`), `FakeChannelPipeline` (no-op pipeline), `FakeConnection` (NMS `Connection` subclass вҖ” overrides all three `send()` variants as no-ops to prevent vanilla connection side-effects during `placeNewPlayer()` that previously caused a phantom "Anonymous User" / UUID-0 ghost entry; `isConnected()` always returns `true`; injects `FakeChannel` and calls `Connection.configureSerialization()` on the no-op pipeline to satisfy NMS internal checks), and `FakeServerGamePacketListenerImpl` (no-op SGPL subclass that discards all outbound packets except knockback). Note: `BotHeadAI` has been removed; head-AI functionality (player tracking, look-range, turn-speed) is now driven by config keys `head-ai.*` and executed inside `FakePlayerManager`. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/messaging/` вҖ” proxy communication (`VelocityChannel`): registers `fpp:main` plugin-messaging channel; wraps outbound messages in a BungeeCord `Forward ALL` envelope so Velocity/BungeeCord re-delivers to every backend. Subchannels: `BOT_SPAWN`, `BOT_DESPAWN`, `BOT_UPDATE` (display-name sync вҖ” wire: `[msgId][serverId][uuid][newDisplayName]`; sent by `broadcastBotDisplayNameUpdate(fp)` when LP refreshes a bot's display name), `CHAT`, `ALERT`, `JOIN`, `LEAVE`, `SYNC`. Echo suppression via per-message unique IDs. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/sync/` вҖ” proxy config sync (`ConfigSyncManager`): push/pull config files to/from MySQL in NETWORK mode. Supports `DISABLED`, `MANUAL`, `AUTO_PULL`, `AUTO_PUSH` modes. Server-specific keys (`database.server-id`, `database.mysql.*`, `debug`) are never synced. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/database/` вҖ” session history and analytics (`DatabaseManager`, `BotRecord`). - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/lang/` вҖ” message rendering (`Lang`). - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/permission/` вҖ” centralised permission registry (`Perm`).
2) Where to start (entry points) - `FakePlayerPlugin.java` вҖ” plugin lifecycle (`onEnable` / `onDisable`) and orchestration. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/command/` вҖ” command classes (`SpawnCommand`, `DeleteCommand` (registers as `despawn`), `ListCommand`, `HelpCommand`, `InfoCommand`, `ChatCommand`, `SwapCommand`, `ReloadCommand`, `FreezeCommand`, `StatsCommand`, `MigrateCommand`, `TpCommand`, `TphCommand`, `LpInfoCommand`, `RankCommand`, `AlertCommand`, `SyncCommand`, `PeaksCommand`, `SettingCommand`, `InventoryCommand`, `MoveCommand`, `XpCommand`, `CmdCommand`, `MineCommand`) - `command/FppCommand.java` вҖ” interface every sub-command implements (`getName`, `getAliases` (default `emptyList()` вҖ” used e.g. by `InventoryCommand` for alias `"inv"`), `getUsage`, `getDescription`, `getPermission`, `canUse`, `execute`, `tabComplete`). - `config/Config.java`, `util/ConfigMigrator.java` вҖ” configuration pattern and automatic migration. - `fakeplayer/FakePlayerManager.java`, `fakeplayer/BotPersistence.java` вҖ” bot lifecycle, persistence and restore. Key new helpers: `requestNavJump(UUID)` вҖ” sets a 5-tick jump-hold for navigation (used by `MoveCommand` to prevent the swim-AI from cancelling jumps before physics fires); `clearNavJump(UUID)` вҖ” clears the pending jump on despawn. - `fakeplayer/BotPathfinder.java` вҖ” server-side A* grid pathfinder for bot navigation (used by `MoveCommand`). Supports 6 `MoveType` actions: `WALK`, `ASCEND`, `DESCEND`, `PARKOUR`, `BREAK`, `PLACE`. Feature flags passed via `PathOptions` record (`parkour`, `breakBlocks`, `placeBlocks`); all default `false`. Key limits: `MAX_NODES=2000` (plain), `MAX_NODES_EXTENDED=4000` (with advanced options), `MAX_RANGE=64` blocks straight-line. Public entry points: `BotPathfinder.findPathMoves(world, sx,sy,sz, tx,ty,tz, opts)` вҶ’ `List<Move>`; `BotPathfinder.findPath(...)` вҶ’ `List<Pos>` (backward-compat, strips action metadata). `walkable(world, x, y, z)` and `passable(world, x, y, z)` are public world-query helpers. - `command/InventoryCommand.java` вҖ” `/fpp inventory <bot>` (alias `inv`). Opens a 54-slot double-chest GUI displaying the bot's full inventory (main storage rows 1-3, hotbar row 4, label bar row 5, equipment+offhand row 6). Implements both `FppCommand` and `Listener`. Equipment slots enforce type restrictions (boots/leggings/chestplate-or-elytra/helmet/offhand). Right-clicking a bot entity directly opens the GUI without a command. `openGui(Player, FakePlayer)` is the public entry point. Registered as a listener in `onEnable` immediately after command registration. Permission: `Perm.INVENTORY = "fpp.inventory"`. - `command/MoveCommand.java` вҖ” `/fpp move <bot> <player>`. Navigates a bot to an online player using `BotPathfinder`. 1-tick repeating task per active navigation; handles WALK/ASCEND/DESCEND/PARKOUR/BREAK/PLACE waypoints. Recalculates path when target moves >3.5 blocks or every 60 ticks. Calls `manager.requestNavJump(UUID)` for ascend/parkour/stuck-recovery jumps. Stuck detection (8 ticks without movement) forces jump + recalc. `cancelAll()` is a public method but is NOT called in `onDisable` (nav tasks cancel themselves on arrival or when the bot/target goes offline). Permission: `Perm.MOVE = "fpp.move"`. - `command/XpCommand.java` вҖ” `/fpp xp <bot>`. Transfers all XP (`getTotalExperience()`) from a bot to the command sender (player only; console gets nothing but XP is still cleared). Clears bot's total XP, level, and progress. Imposes a 30-second post-collection cooldown on the bot's XP pickup (`BotXpPickupListener` reads `plugin.getXpCommand().isOnXpCooldown(uuid)`). Exposed via `plugin.getXpCommand()`. Permission: `Perm.XP = "fpp.user.xp"` (user-tier, included in `fpp.user.*`). - `command/CmdCommand.java` вҖ” `/fpp cmd <bot> <command...>` (direct execution), `/fpp cmd <bot> --add <command...>` (store right-click command), `/fpp cmd <bot> --clear` (remove stored command), `/fpp cmd <bot> --show` (display stored command). Uses `Bukkit.dispatchCommand()` which bypasses `PlayerCommandPreprocessEvent` so `BotCommandBlocker` does not interfere. Right-click behavior handled in `InventoryCommand.onRightClickBot()` вҖ” bots with stored commands execute them instead of opening inventory GUI. Permission: `Perm.CMD = "fpp.cmd"`. - `command/MineCommand.java` вҖ” `/fpp mine <bot>` (continuous mining), `/fpp mine <bot> once` (mine one block), `/fpp mine <bot> stop` (stop mining), `/fpp mine stop` (stop all bots). Bots continuously break blocks they're looking at using NMS `handleBlockBreakAction()` and `destroyBlockProgress()` packets. Creative mode: instant break with 5-tick cooldown. Survival mode: progressive mining with destroy-speed accumulation. Mining state tracked per-bot (`MiningState` вҖ” `currentPos`, `progress`, `freeze`, `once`). 1-tick repeating task per active miner; tasks auto-cancel when bot/target goes offline. Public API: `stopMining(UUID)`, `stopAll()`. Permission: `Perm.MINE = "fpp.mine"`. - `fakeplayer/PeakHoursManager.java` вҖ” time-window-based bot pool scheduler. Runs a 60-second repeating tick; computes a target fraction of the total bot pool that should be online based on the current time window (`peak-hours.schedule` / `peak-hours.day-overrides`), then gradually sleeps (quietly removes) or wakes (respawns) AFK bots to match that fraction. Stagger spread is configured via `peak-hours.stagger-seconds`. Requires `swap.enabled: true`. Integrates with `BotSwapAI` вҖ” swapped-out bots count toward the pool so short swap absences don't distort the math. Sleeping-bot state is persisted crash-safely via `fpp_sleeping_bots` DB table (populated on every queue mutation; cleared on `wakeAll()`). Crash recovery: `restoreSleepingBotsFromDatabase(db)` repopulates the in-memory deque at startup. Hot-reload: `reload()` wakes all sleeping bots then restarts the tick if still enabled. Key public API: `start()`, `shutdown()`, `reload()`, `wakeAll()`, `wakeBotByName(name)`, `putBotToSleepByName(name)`, `forceCheck()`, `getSleepingCount()`, `getTotalPool()`, `getSleepingEntries()`, `getSleepingNames()`, `isSleeping(name)`, `getCurrentWindowLabel()`, `computeTargetFraction()`, `getSecondsToNextWindow()`, `getNextWindowFraction()`, `isRunning()`. Accessed via `plugin.getPeakHoursManager()`. Inner record: `PeakHoursManager.SleepingBot(name, loc)`. - `fakeplayer/FakePlayer.java` вҖ” domain object for a single active bot (name, UUID, profile, spawnLocation, displayName, skinName, frozen state). `getPhysicsEntity()` and `getPlayer()` both return the single NMS ServerPlayer entity. Additional fields: `luckpermsGroup` (per-bot LP group override, `null` = not yet resolved; get/set via `getLuckpermsGroup()`/`setLuckpermsGroup()`), `rawDisplayName` (the `{bot_name}` slot before LP prefix/suffix are added; used as the stable base for `refreshLpDisplayName` rebuilds), `bodyless` (when `true` the bot exists in tab-list/chat only, no entity in world вҖ” used for console spawns without location data), `botType` (`BotType` enum вҖ” `AFK` = default passive bot; `PVP` = PvP bot (name prefixed `pvp_<name>`, currently restricted to internal dev UUID); get/set via `getBotType()`/`setBotType()`), `chatEnabled` (per-bot chat mute flag, default `true`; `false` = permanently silenced; get/set via `isChatEnabled()`/`setChatEnabled()`), `chatTier` (per-bot chat activity tier override, `null` = random; values `"quiet"`, `"passive"`, `"normal"`, `"active"`, `"chatty"`; get/set via `getChatTier()`/`setChatTier()`). Note: `botIndex` has been removed; `nametagEntity` remains as a legacy-compat field (no-op for NMS players). - `fakeplayer/FakePlayerBody.java` вҖ” NMS ServerPlayer spawning/removal. Entities tagged via PDC keys for identification. - `fakeplayer/NmsPlayerSpawner.java` вҖ” reflection-based NMS `ServerPlayer` factory (cached class/method/constructor lookups); bypasses Mojang auth by creating the player directly in JVM memory. - `fakeplayer/NmsReflection.java` вҖ” shared NMS reflection bootstrap (CraftBukkit/NMS class resolution); initialised once, thread-safe. - `fakeplayer/RemoteBotCache.java` вҖ” thread-safe registry of bots physically running on other proxy servers. Always initialised (even in LOCAL mode). Populated from DB at startup (NETWORK mode) and updated via `BOT_SPAWN`/`BOT_DESPAWN` plugin messages. Exposed via `FakePlayerPlugin.getRemoteBotCache()`. - `fakeplayer/RemoteBotEntry.java` вҖ” immutable record (`serverId`, `uuid`, `name`, `displayName`, `packetProfileName`, `skinValue`, `skinSignature`) for a remote bot; `hasSkin()` checks whether texture data is present. - `fakeplayer/SkinProfile.java` вҖ” immutable snapshot of a resolved Mojang skin (base64 value + signature + source label). - `config/BotNameConfig.java`, `config/BotMessageConfig.java` вҖ” bot name & message pools which are initialised in `onEnable`. - `fakeplayer/SkinRepository.java` / `fakeplayer/SkinFetcher.java` вҖ” skin-loading subsystem (lazy singleton; explicitly initialised via `SkinRepository.get().init(this)` in `onEnable` after BotMessageConfig but before database init; must be ready before any bot spawn including persistence restore). - `fakeplayer/network/FakeConnection.java` вҖ” NMS `Connection` subclass used for every bot. Created before `placeNewPlayer()` via `new FakeConnection(loopbackAddress)`; sets `this.channel = new FakeChannel(...)` and calls `Connection.configureSerialization()` on the no-op pipeline. Overrides all three `send()` overloads as no-ops to eliminate vanilla connection side-effects (protocol-state registers, pending-ack queues, callback notifications) that caused a phantom "Anonymous User" entry (UUID `0000вҖҰ0000`) in the player list. `isConnected()` always returns `true` so NMS never treats the bot as disconnected. - `fakeplayer/network/FakeServerGamePacketListenerImpl.java` вҖ” extends NMS `ServerGamePacketListenerImpl`; `send()` is a no-op that discards all outbound packets. Exceptions: `ClientboundSetEntityMotionPacket` (knockback) is intercepted and applied server-side via reflected `getMovement()`/`lerpMotion()` (1.21.9+) or `getXa()/Ya()/Za()` fallback. Injected into `ServerPlayer.connection` immediately after `placeNewPlayer()` so `awaitingPositionFromClient` stays `null` вҖ” preventing the server from snapping the bot back to its spawn position. - `fakeplayer/SkinProfileInjector.java` вҖ” reflection helper that injects a `textures` property into an authlib `GameProfile`; used by both NMS body spawns and tab-list packet profiles for consistent skin application. Call `SkinProfileInjector.apply(gameProfile, skinProfile)`. - `fakeplayer/BotIdentityCache.java` вҖ” persistent registry mapping `(bot_name, server_id) вҶ’ stable UUID`. Ensures a bot with the same name always rejoins with the same UUID (LuckPerms data, inventory, session history stay linked). Storage priority: (1) in-memory `ConcurrentHashMap` (always checked first), (2) `fpp_bot_identities` DB table when DB is enabled, (3) `data/bot-identities.yml` YAML fallback when DB is disabled. Initialized in `onEnable` after the DB, set on `FakePlayerManager` via `setIdentityCache()`; `FakePlayerManager.resolveUuid(name)` delegates here for every fresh spawn. `prime(name, uuid)` pre-populates the cache from `spawnRestored()` so restart-restored bots don't hit the DB a second time. **Identity scope:** per-server вҖ” cross-server UUID sharing is intentionally not supported. Keys are lowercased for case-insensitive lookups. - `gui/SettingGui.java` вҖ” interactive 3-row chest GUI (`/fpp settings`). 7 categories: General, Body, Chat, Swap, Peak Hours, PvP, Pathfinding; up to 18 settings per page. Toggle items flip their boolean in-place on click; numeric/double items close the chest and prompt the player to type a value in chat (message cancelled вҖ” invisible to other players, 60-second timeout auto-cancels). After each change, calls `plugin.saveConfig()`, `Config.reload()`, and `applyLiveEffect(configKey)` вҖ” which mirrors `ReloadCommand`'s subsystem calls for instant live effect without a full `/fpp reload`. **Reset button** (slot 18): resets all settings on the current page to JAR defaults. Per-player session state stored in `sessions` map (keyed by UUID). Chat-input sessions tracked separately in `chatSessions`; `pendingChatInput` / `pendingRebuild` sets prevent false `InventoryCloseEvent` handling during transitions. `SettingGui` is a singleton instantiated in `onEnable`, passed to `SettingCommand`, and registered as a listener via `registerEvents(settingGui, this)`. **Permission:** `Perm.SETTINGS = "fpp.settings"` (default `op`). - `lang/Lang.java` вҖ” message rendering. Loads `language/en.yml`, supports named and positional placeholders. Use `Lang.get("key", "name", "value")` for an Adventure `Component` or `Lang.raw("key", ...)` for a raw string. Hot-reloadable via `/fpp reload`. - `permission/Perm.java` вҖ” central permission registry. All permission nodes declared as constants (e.g. `Perm.SPAWN`, `Perm.DELETE`, `Perm.USER_SPAWN`). Use `Perm.has()`, `Perm.hasOrOp()`, `Perm.resolveUserBotLimit()` helpers. Never hard-code permission strings in commands. - `database/DatabaseManager.java`, `database/BotRecord.java` вҖ” session history and analytics. Supports SQLite (default, WAL mode) and MySQL. Writes are serialised on a background thread; positions are batch-flushed on a configurable interval. Controlled by `Config.databaseEnabled()` and `Config.mysqlEnabled()`. **Schema version 8.** Tables: `fpp_bot_sessions` (session history), `fpp_active_bots` (restart persistence source of truth), `fpp_sleeping_bots` (peak-hours crash-safe sleeping-bot queue вҖ” `sleep_order`, `bot_name`, `world_name`, `pos_x/y/z`, `pos_yaw/pitch`, `server_id`; populated whenever the peak-hours sleep queue changes, cleared on `wakeAll()`), `fpp_bot_identities` (stable nameвҶ’UUID registry вҖ” `bot_name`, `server_id`, `bot_uuid`, `created_at`; PRIMARY KEY `(bot_name, server_id)`; backfilled from earliest session history on schema upgrade), `fpp_meta` (schema version). API: `saveSleepingBots(List<SleepingBotRow>)`, `loadSleepingBots()`, `clearSleepingBots()`, `lookupBotUuid(botName, serverId)` (synchronous read вҶ’ `UUID` or `null`), `registerBotUuid(botName, uuid, serverId)` (enqueued write вҖ” INSERT OR IGNORE so first UUID always wins). - `util/TabListManager.java`, `util/UpdateChecker.java` вҖ” tab header/footer management and async update checker initialized during startup.
3) Key patterns & concrete examples (copy-ready pointers) - Migration runs before config init: `ConfigMigrator.migrateIfNeeded(this)` in `onEnable` (see `FakePlayerPlugin.java`). - Config usage: call `Config.init(this)` then use accessors like `Config.fakeChatEnabled()` in code. Tab-list visibility accessor: `Config.tabListEnabled()` (key `tab-list.enabled`, default `true`) вҖ” when `false`, bots are never added to any player's tab list; the 20-tick display-name refresh loop, `syncToPlayer`, and the respawn re-add in `FakePlayerEntityListener` all check this flag before sending any `sendTabListAdd` packets. Toggling via `/fpp reload` calls `FakePlayerManager.applyTabListConfig()` which immediately removes or re-adds all active bots' tab entries for all online players. **Key accessors:** `Config.fakeChatEnabled()` (`fake-chat.enabled`, default `false`); `Config.fakeChatRequirePlayer()` (`fake-chat.require-player-online`, default `true`); `Config.fakeChatMessages()` (messages from `bot-messages.yml`). Local bots with a body use `player.chat(message)` so the server's real chat pipeline handles formatting.
