Bodyless bots and remote proxy bots use `Config.fakeChatRemoteFormat()` (`fake-chat.remote-format`, default `"<yellow>{name}<dark_gray>: <white>{message}"`) вҖ” supports `{name}` and `{message}` placeholders. `Config.bodyPushable()` (`body.pushable`, default `true`); `Config.bodyDamageable()` (`body.damageable`, default `true`); `Config.bodyPickUpItems()` (`body.pick-up-items`, default `false` вҖ” bots do not pick up items); `Config.bodyPickUpXp()` (`body.pick-up-xp`, default `true` вҖ” bots pick up XP orbs; set `false` to prevent all orb pickup; also honoured by the `XpCommand` post-collection cooldown via `BotXpPickupListener`); `Config.warningsNotifyAdmins()` (`messages.notify-admins-on-join`, default `true`) вҖ” send compatibility warning to online admins on startup. **Swap system config (v1.5.17):** `Config.swapMinOnline()` (`swap.min-online`, default `0` вҖ” minimum bots that must stay online; swap skips if removing one would drop below this floor); `Config.swapRetryRejoin()` (`swap.retry-rejoin`, default `true` вҖ” auto-retry failed rejoins); `Config.swapRetryDelay()` (`swap.retry-delay`, default `60` seconds вҖ” delay before retry). **Granular debug flags:** `Config.debugStartup()`, `Config.debugNms()`, `Config.debugPackets()`, `Config.debugLuckPerms()`, `Config.debugNetwork()`, `Config.debugConfigSync()`, `Config.debugSkin()`, `Config.debugDatabase()`, `Config.debugChat()`, `Config.debugSwap()` вҖ” each maps to `logging.debug.<category>` in config (also triggered by global `debug: true`). **Network/proxy accessors:** `Config.databaseMode()` (`database.mode`, `"LOCAL"` or `"NETWORK"`); `Config.isNetworkMode()` вҖ” shorthand for `databaseEnabled() && databaseMode()=="NETWORK"`; `Config.serverId()` (`database.server-id`, falls back to legacy `server.id`); `Config.configSyncMode()` (`config-sync.mode`, one of `DISABLED` / `MANUAL` / `AUTO_PULL` / `AUTO_PUSH`). Server-specific keys (`database.server-id`, `database.mysql.*`, `debug`) are never pushed/pulled by `ConfigSyncManager`. **Performance config (v1.5.17):** `Config.positionSyncDistance()` (`performance.position-sync-distance`, default `128.0` вҖ” max distance for position sync packets; `0` = unlimited). Note: `Config.tabListNameFormat()` and the `bot-name.tab-list-format` config key were **removed** in config v38 вҖ” LP manages prefix/suffix natively for NMS ServerPlayer entities and the display name packet carries only the bot name itself. - Language messages: always use `Lang.get("key")` (returns Adventure `Component`) or `Lang.raw("key")` (returns `String`) вҖ” never hard-code user-facing text. Named placeholders: `Lang.get("freeze-frozen", "name", fp.getDisplayName())`. All keys live in `src/main/resources/language/en.yml`. - Permission checks: use `Perm.has(sender, Perm.SPAWN)` or `Perm.hasOrOp(sender, Perm.OP)` вҖ” never hard-code node strings. **All permissions declared in `Perm.java` as constants AND in `plugin.yml` for LuckPerms auto-discovery.** Per-user bot limits resolved via `Perm.resolveUserBotLimit(sender)` (scans `fpp.spawn.limit.1` вҖҰ `fpp.spawn.limit.100`; returns -1 if none set, caller falls back to `Config.userBotLimit()`). **Permission structure:** `fpp.op` grants all commands (admin wildcard, default: op); `fpp.use` grants user-tier commands: spawn (with 1-bot limit), tph, xp, info-user (default: true вҖ” all players). Bot limits assigned via `fpp.spawn.limit.<num>` nodes (1-100); `fpp.spawn.limit.1` is included in `fpp.use` by default. New command permission constants: `Perm.INVENTORY = "fpp.inventory"`, `Perm.MOVE = "fpp.move"`, `Perm.USER_XP = "fpp.xp"` (user-tier, included in `fpp.use`), `Perm.USER_INFO = "fpp.info.user"`, `Perm.USER_TPH = "fpp.tph"`, `Perm.MIGRATE = "fpp.migrate"`. When adding new commands: (1) add constant to `Perm.java`, (2) add full entry to `plugin.yml` with description and default, (3) add to `fpp.op` children list in `plugin.yml`, (4) use constant in command's `getPermission()` and `canUse()` methods. **LuckPerms tab-completion depends on `plugin.yml` declarations** вҖ” missing entries won't appear in `/lp permission set fpp.<tab>`. - Command registration: plugin registers commands via `commandManager.register(new SpawnCommand(...))`; `HelpCommand` is auto-registered inside `CommandManager`, and `/fpp help` reads live from the registered command list, so new sub-commands need no separate help-menu wiring. The plugin then calls `getCommand("fpp").setExecutor(commandManager)` (see `onEnable`). - Listener registration: `getServer().getPluginManager().registerEvents(new PlayerJoinListener(this, fakePlayerManager), this)` вҖ” listeners receive plugin + manager references. - Persistence lifecycle: restore on enable via `botPersistence.purgeOrphanedBodiesAndRestore(fakePlayerManager)` (purges orphaned entities then calls `restore()` 5 ticks later) and save on disable via `botPersistence.save(fakePlayerManager.getActivePlayers())`. `BotPersistence.saveAsync()` also available for non-shutdown saves (snapshots locations on main thread, writes async). When DB is enabled, the `fpp_active_bots` table is the **primary** restore source (`DatabaseManager.getActiveBots()`); `data/active-bots.yml` is the fallback for no-DB or empty-table cases. - **Performance optimization (v1.5.17):** `FakePlayerManager.getByName()` is now O(1) via secondary `nameIndex` map (`ConcurrentHashMap<String, FakePlayer>`, lowercased keys) вҖ” was O(n) linear scan. The index is maintained at all add/remove sites. Additionally, position sync packets are distance-culled via `Config.positionSyncDistance()` (`performance.position-sync-distance`, default `128.0` blocks, `0` = unlimited) вҖ” only players within range receive per-tick position updates, reducing packet overhead on large servers. - Metrics & soft-deps: metrics are initialized with `FppMetrics` when `Config.metricsEnabled()`; PlaceholderAPI expansion registered conditionally in `onEnable`. - Compatibility checks: `CompatibilityChecker.check()` (in `util/CompatibilityChecker.java`) **always returns a non-restricted result** вҖ” the old Paper-detection and minimum MC version gate has been removed. `check()` is **not called in `onEnable`**; `compatibilityRestricted` and `compatibilityWarningMessage` fields have been removed from `FakePlayerPlugin`. The class retains two general-purpose utilities: `CompatibilityChecker.extractMcVersion()` (returns detected MC version string) and `CompatibilityChecker.isVersionAtLeast(version, required)` (component-wise numeric comparison). `buildWarningComponent(result)` always returns `null`. **Version gate (separate from `check()`):** `FakePlayerPlugin` directly calls `CompatibilityChecker.isVersionAtLeast(detectedMcVersion, "1.21.12")` in `onEnable` to set `versionUnsupported = true` when the server runs MC 1.21.12+. New fields: `versionUnsupported` (boolean) and `detectedMcVersion` (String); new accessors: `plugin.isVersionUnsupported()` and `plugin.getDetectedMcVersion()`. When `versionUnsupported = true`: `CommandManager` blocks all sub-commands (bare `/fpp` still shows plugin info), `PlayerJoinListener` sends the `"version-unsupported-admin"` Lang warning to operators on every join, and a prominent banner is printed to the console at startup. FPP is tested up to MC 1.21.11; any newer version triggers this gate. - Bot pools: `BotNameConfig.init(this)` and `BotMessageConfig.init(this)` are called during startup вҖ” the plugin loads `bot-names.yml` and `bot-messages.yml` early. - Metrics / FastStats packaging: `FppMetrics` is initialized before the startup banner to reflect metrics status. FastStats jars are bundled under `src/main/resources/faststats/` (the POM treats them as binary resources, not shaded). Note: building requires JDK 21 (see `pom.xml` <java.version>21). - Dependencies: **PacketEvents 2.x is required** вҖ” FPP uses it for tab-list packet integration. Declared in `plugin.yml` as a soft-depend: `PlaceholderAPI` and `LuckPerms` (both optional, auto-detected). - Entity stack: each bot is a single NMS ServerPlayer entity (created via `NmsPlayerSpawner`) with full player functionality including chat, tab-list presence, and realistic movement. Use PDC keys for identification during event handling. - Database gate: check `Config.databaseEnabled()` before any `DatabaseManager` call; `databaseManager` may be `null` at runtime (disabled or failed to init). `databaseManager.recordAllShutdown()` must be called in `onDisable` before `databaseManager.close()`. - Text formatting: `TextUtil.colorize(string)` parses MiniMessage tags and legacy `&`/`В§` codes into an Adventure `Component`. `TextUtil.format(string)` additionally applies small-caps Unicode conversion. **Supports all modern color formats:** MiniMessage tags (`<rainbow>`, `<gradient:#FF0000:#0000FF>`), hex colors (`<#9782ff>`), LuckPerms gradient shorthand (`{#fffff>}text{#00000<}`), and mixed formats (`&7[<#9782ff>Phantom</#9782ff>&7]`). - LuckPerms integration: `LuckPermsHelper` reads resolved prefix/suffix directly from LP's in-memory `CachedMetaData` вҖ” bots are real NMS `ServerPlayer` entities, LP handles them natively. Core helpers: `LuckPermsHelper.isAvailable()`, `LuckPermsHelper.groupExists(groupName)`, `LuckPermsHelper.getAllGroupNames()`, `LuckPermsHelper.buildGroupSummary()`. Runtime group assignment: `LuckPermsHelper.setPlayerGroup(uuid, groupName)` (works online or offline) or `LuckPermsHelper.applyGroupToOnlineUser(uuid, groupName)` (prefers the live `User` instance so `UserDataRecalculateEvent` fires immediately вҖ” preferred when bot is already spawned). Prefix/suffix reads: `LuckPermsHelper.getResolvedPrefix(uuid)` / `LuckPermsHelper.getResolvedSuffix(uuid)` (synchronous, requires bot to be online). Async group read from storage: `LuckPermsHelper.getStoredPrimaryGroup(uuid)`. Online-only read: `LuckPermsHelper.getPrimaryGroup(uuid)`. Force LP metadata refresh: `LuckPermsHelper.refreshUserCache(uuid)`. `LuckPermsHelper.invalidateCache()` is `@Deprecated` (no-op вҖ” LP handles caching natively). **Event subscription:** `LuckPermsHelper.subscribeLpEvents(plugin, manager)` subscribes to `UserDataRecalculateEvent` so bot display names auto-update whenever a group changes (LP command, API, etc.); called directly in `onEnable` (not deferred). `LuckPermsHelper.unsubscribeLpEvents()` is called in `onDisable` to prevent memory leaks. **LP group assignment:** `Config.luckpermsDefaultGroup()` (`luckperms.default-group`, default `""`) вҖ” optional LP group assigned at spawn; blank = `"default"`. Use `/fpp lpinfo [bot-name]` for in-game diagnostics. Check LP availability via `LuckPermsHelper.isAvailable()` before any API call. **LP injection flow:** `finishSpawn` calls `LuckPermsHelper.ensureGroupBeforeSpawn(botUuid, cfgGroup)` **async before the NMS body spawns** вҖ” writes the group to LP storage so `PlayerJoinEvent` listeners (TAB plugin, etc.) see the correct group immediately. If the bot already has a non-default group in storage (prior `/fpp rank`), that group is preserved unless `luckperms.default-group` overrides it. Once LP completes, `spawnBodyAndFinish(fp, spawnLoc)` runs on the main thread. After the body spawns, `LuckPermsHelper.applyGroupToOnlineUser(uuid, group)` is called 5 ticks later; after that completes, `refreshLpDisplayName(fp)` is called 2 ticks later to update the tab-list display name. `FakePlayer.rawDisplayName` is the stable `{bot_name}` base for rebuilds. Call `FakePlayerManager.refreshLpDisplayName(fp)` to manually rebuild display name + resend tab-list update packets. **Scoreboard team override:** All bots are placed in the `~fpp` scoreboard team (managed by `BotTabTeam`) which sorts AFTER all other teams, ensuring bots always appear below real players regardless of LP group weight. Note: `LpData` record, `getBotLpData()`, `buildPacketProfileName()`, `invalidateGroup()`, `refreshRankList()`, and the packet-prefix-char weight-rank ordering system have all been removed. **ClassLoader guard:** `FakePlayerPlugin` caches a `luckPermsAvailable` boolean (set once in `onEnable()` via `Bukkit.getPluginManager().getPlugin("LuckPerms") != null`) accessible via `plugin.isLuckPermsAvailable()`. **Never reference `LuckPermsHelper` or any LP API class unless this returns `true`** вҖ” Paper's `PluginClassLoader` eagerly resolves LP API class references during bytecode verification, so even calling `LuckPermsHelper.isAvailable()` on a server without LP installed throws `NoClassDefFoundError`. The pattern everywhere LP is needed: `if (plugin.isLuckPermsAvailable()) { LuckPermsHelper.doSomething(...); }`. - WorldGuard integration: `WorldGuardHelper` (in `util/WorldGuardHelper.java`) provides PvP region protection for bots. Key methods: `WorldGuardHelper.isPvpAllowed(location)` вҖ” queries active WG region flags; returns `true` when no region explicitly sets PVP to DENY (fail-open: unprotected areas always allow PvP, only explicit DENY blocks damage); `WorldGuardHelper.findSafeLocation(world)` вҖ” returns the world spawn if PvP is allowed there, otherwise `null`. Used by `FakePlayerEntityListener` at `LOWEST` priority to cancel player-sourced PvP damage to bots inside no-PvP regions. **ClassLoader guard:** `FakePlayerPlugin` caches `worldGuardAvailable` (set once via `Bukkit.getPluginManager().getPlugin("WorldGuard") != null`) accessible via `plugin.isWorldGuardAvailable()`. **Never reference `WorldGuardHelper` unless this returns `true`** вҖ” same NoClassDefFoundError risk as LuckPerms. Pattern: `if (plugin.isWorldGuardAvailable()) { WorldGuardHelper.doSomething(...); }`. `WorldGuard` is declared in `plugin.yml` as a soft-depend. - Listener registration: `getServer().getPluginManager().registerEvents(new PlayerJoinListener(this, fakePlayerManager), this)` вҖ” listeners receive plugin + manager references. `FakePlayerKickListener` (guards all bots from automated kicks like anti-idle/anti-AFK) is always registered; all other listeners (`FakePlayerEntityListener`, `BotCollisionListener`, etc.) are also always registered вҖ” the old `compatibilityRestricted` skip is gone. - `onDisable` sequence: `chunkLoader.releaseAll()` вҶ’ `botChatAI.cancelAll()` вҶ’ **`peakHoursManager.shutdown()`** (wakes all sleeping bots so they appear in `activePlayers`) вҶ’ `botSwapAI.cancelAll()` вҶ’ **save persistence** (sleeping bots are now active and captured) вҶ’ `LuckPermsHelper.unsubscribeLpEvents()` вҶ’ `fakePlayerManager.removeAllSync()` (also calls `pvpAI.cancelAll()` internally) вҶ’ `tabListManager.shutdown()` вҶ’ `botTabTeam.destroy()` вҶ’ `databaseManager.recordAllShutdown()` + `close()` вҶ’ `fppMetrics.shutdown()` вҶ’ unregister VelocityChannel messaging channels. **Critical ordering:** `peakHoursManager.shutdown()` MUST run before `botPersistence.save()` so sleeping bots are woken and included in the persistence file. - Update notifications to late-joining admins: stored as a `Component` on `FakePlayerPlugin.getUpdateNotification()` / `setUpdateNotification()`; `PlayerJoinListener` delivers it to players with `Perm.ALL`. - YAML file sync: `YamlFileSyncer.syncMissingKeys(plugin, diskRelPath, jarResourcePath)` вҖ” non-destructive; adds keys present in the JAR but absent from disk, never overwrites user values. Returns a `SyncResult` record (`fileName`, `keysAdded`, `hasChanges()`, `count()`). Called automatically inside `Lang.reload()`, `BotNameConfig.reload()`, and `BotMessageConfig.reload()` on every startup and `/fpp reload`. Force-run from the command line with `/fpp migrate lang`, `/fpp migrate names`, or `/fpp migrate messages`. When adding new keys to `en.yml`, `bot-names.yml`, or `bot-messages.yml`, they will be automatically synced to existing user files on next reload вҖ” no manual migration step needed. - Spawn cooldown: `Config.spawnCooldown()` (key `spawn-cooldown`, default `0` = disabled). Applies to both admin and user `/fpp spawn`. Players with `Perm.BYPASS_COOLDOWN` (`fpp.bypass.cooldown`) are exempt. Cooldown state lives in `FakePlayerManager.spawnCooldowns` (per-UUID map). Helpers: `manager.isOnCooldown(uuid)`, `manager.getRemainingCooldown(uuid)` (seconds), `manager.recordSpawnCooldown(uuid)`. - Backup variants: `BackupManager.createFullBackup(plugin, reason)` вҖ” backs up all plugin files including SQLite; used by `ConfigMigrator` before migrations. `BackupManager.createConfigFilesBackup(plugin, reason)` вҖ” backs up YAML files only (`config.yml`, `bot-names.yml`, `bot-messages.yml`, `language/`); used before lightweight sync operations (e.g. `/fpp migrate lang`). Both methods prune to the most recent `MAX_BACKUPS` (10) automatically. - Additional `Perm` helpers: `Perm.missing(sender, node)` returns `true` when the sender lacks the node (negation of `has()`); `Perm.hasAny(sender, node1, node2, ...)` returns `true` when the sender has at least one of the listed nodes. - Physical-body API: `FakePlayerManager.physicalBodiesEnabled()` вҖ” returns `Config.spawnBody()`. (The old `&& !plugin.isCompatibilityRestricted()` check has been removed.) Use this instead of checking `Config.spawnBody()` inline when deciding whether NMS ServerPlayer bodies are active. - Display-name pipeline: `FakePlayerManager.finalizeDisplayName(rawName, botName)` вҖ” private helper that expands PAPI `%placeholders%` via `PlaceholderAPI.setPlaceholders(null, ...)` (null player = server-wide), then calls `sanitizeDisplayName()`. **Note:** the `{prefix}/{bot_name}/{suffix}` tab-list format substitution (previously driven by `bot-name.tab-list-format`) was removed in config v38 вҖ” LP manages prefix/suffix natively for real NMS ServerPlayer entities; the display name packet carries only the bot name. Called at every display-name build site: `spawnUserBot`, `spawn`, `spawnRestored`, `validateUserBotNames`. Public entry point for post-LP-assignment rebuilds: `FakePlayerManager.refreshLpDisplayName(fp)` (also has a private retry variant `refreshLpDisplayNameWithRetry` for LP metadata race conditions). - Body damageable/pushable live-reload: `FakePlayerManager.applyBodyConfig()` вҖ” called on `/fpp reload`; handles damage/pushable configuration for all active NMS ServerPlayer entities. `BotCollisionListener` also guards all three push paths (`onEntityDamageByEntity`, `onPlayerMove`, `tickBotSeparation`) with `if (!Config.bodyPushable()) return;`. - Swim AI: `Config.swimAiEnabled()` (`swim-ai.enabled`, default `true`). Each tick the main loop calls `NmsPlayerSpawner.setJumping(bot, bot.isInWater() || bot.isInLava())` so bots automatically swim upward when submerged, mimicking a player holding spacebar. Set `swim-ai.enabled: false` to let bots sink/drown instead. - Pathfinding config (used by `/fpp move`): `Config.pathfindingParkour()` (`pathfinding.parkour`, default `false`); `Config.pathfindingBreakBlocks()` (`pathfinding.break-blocks`, default `false`); `Config.pathfindingPlaceBlocks()` (`pathfinding.place-blocks`, default `false`); `Config.pathfindingPlaceMaterial()` (`pathfinding.place-material`, default `"DIRT"` вҖ” material placed when bridging gaps; falls back to `DIRT` if the value is not a valid solid block). These correspond to `BotPathfinder.PathOptions` fields and are re-read on every path recalculation. - PVP AI subsystem: `BotPvpAI` drives all movement and combat for `BotType.PVP` bots. Initialized in `FakePlayerManager` constructor; accessed via `manager.getPvpAI()`. The main tick loop calls `pvpAI.tickBot(fp, bot, online)` each game tick for PVP bots only (head-AI is skipped for PVP bots вҖ” `BotPvpAI` handles rotation internally). Key config: `Config.pvpAiDifficulty()` (`pvp-ai.difficulty`, default `"medium"`, values: `easy`/`medium`/`hard`/`tier1`/`hacker`); `Config.pvpAiDefensiveMode()` (`pvp-ai.defensive-mode`, default `true` вҖ” bots only attack back when hit); `Config.pvpAiDetectRange()` (`pvp-ai.detect-range`, default `32.0`). When a player damages a PVP bot, `FakePlayerEntityListener` calls `manager.getPvpAI().onBotAttacked(botUuid, attackerUuid)` to register the attacker for defensive retaliation. Combat mode (`BotCombatMode.CRYSTAL`/`SWORD`/`FIST`) is auto-detected from the bot's inventory every 2 seconds. Difficulty controls attack range, crit/s-tap consistency, and early-swing window. **вҡ PVP bots are currently restricted to the internal developer UUID вҖ” not yet available for general spawning.** - BotChatAI expanded features: Each bot is assigned a random activity tier at first use вҖ” quiet (15%), passive (25%), normal (30%), active (18%), chatty (12%) вҖ” which scales the chat interval multiplier. Override per-bot via `FakePlayer.setChatTier(String)` or via `/fpp chat <bot> tier <tier>`; reset to random with `tier reset`. Silence a bot permanently (`/fpp chat <bot> off` or `fp.setChatEnabled(false)`) or temporarily (`/fpp chat <bot> mute [seconds]` вҖ” `BotChatAI.timedMute(uuid, seconds)`). Inspect state with `/fpp chat <bot> info` (shows enabled, tier, multiplier). Bulk operations: `/fpp chat all <on|off|tier <tier>|mute [seconds]>`. **Chat realism config (v39вҶ’v40):** `Config.fakeChatTypingDelay()` (`fake-chat.typing-delay`, default `true`), `Config.fakeChatBurstChance()` (`fake-chat.burst-chance`, default `0.12`), `Config.fakeChatBurstDelayMin()/Max()` (`fake-chat.burst-delay.min/max`, default `2/5`), `Config.fakeChatReplyToMentions()` (`fake-chat.reply-to-mentions`, default `true`), `Config.fakeChatMentionReplyChance()` (`fake-chat.mention-reply-chance`, default `0.65`), `Config.fakeChatReplyDelayMin()/Max()` (`fake-chat.reply-delay.min/max`, default `2/8`), `Config.fakeChatStaggerInterval()` (`fake-chat.stagger-interval`, default `3`), `Config.fakeChatActivityVariation()` (`fake-chat.activity-variation`, default `true`), `Config.fakeChatHistorySize()` (`fake-chat.history-size`, default `5`). Event-triggered chat config: `fake-chat.event-triggers.enabled`, `.on-player-join.*`, `.on-death.*`, `.on-player-leave.*`. Keyword reactions: `fake-chat.keyword-reactions`. **`BotMessageConfig` message pools** (all in `bot-messages.yml`, auto-synced via `YamlFileSyncer`): `messages` (base chat pool), `replies` (mention-reply pool вҖ” `Config.chatReplyMessages()`), `burst-followups` (burst follow-up pool вҖ” `Config.chatBurstMessages()`), `join-reactions` / `death-reactions` / `leave-reactions` / `keyword-reactions.<key>` (event-triggered pools вҖ” `Config.chatJoinReactionMessages()`, `chatDeathReactionMessages()`, `chatLeaveReactionMessages()`, `chatKeywordReactionMessages(key)`). Accessed via `FakePlayerPlugin.getBotChatAI()` or `FakePlayerManager.getBotChatAI()`. - Peak-hours subsystem: `PeakHoursManager` controls time-window-based bot pool scheduling. Requires `swap.enabled: true`. **Config accessors:** `Config.peakHoursEnabled()` (`peak-hours.enabled`, default `false`); `Config.peakHoursTimezone()` (`peak-hours.timezone`, default `"UTC"`); `Config.peakHoursStaggerSeconds()` (`peak-hours.stagger-seconds`, default `30`); `Config.peakHoursSchedule()` (`peak-hours.schedule` вҖ” list of `{start, end, fraction}` maps); `Config.peakHoursDayOverrides()` (`peak-hours.day-overrides` вҖ” per-`DayOfWeek` schedule overrides, keyed by uppercase `DayOfWeek` name); `Config.peakHoursMinOnline()` (`peak-hours.min-online`, default `0` = no floor); `Config.peakHoursNotifyTransitions()` (`peak-hours.notify-transitions`, default `false` вҖ” broadcasts window changes to players with `Perm.PEAKS`). **Permission:** `Perm.PEAKS = "fpp.peaks"`. **Command:** `/fpp peaks [on|off|status|next|force|list|wake [name]|sleep <name>]` вҖ” toggle, inspect, or manually override the sleeping pool. **Hot-reload:** `PeakHoursManager.reload()` is called by `ReloadCommand` (step 3b) вҖ” wakes all sleeping bots, resets transition state, then restarts the tick if still enabled. **`onDisable` ordering:** `peakHoursManager.shutdown()` wakes all sleeping bots BEFORE `botPersistence.save()` so the full pool is captured. **Crash recovery:** `peakHoursManager.restoreSleepingBotsFromDatabase(db)` is called after `botPersistence.purgeOrphanedBodiesAndRestore()` to repopulate the sleeping-bot deque from `fpp_sleeping_bots`; only runs when `databaseManager != null && Config.persistOnRestart()`. - `FakePlayer.getLiveLocation()` вҖ” returns the most accurate current location: the live NMS Player body position when valid, otherwise the last recorded `spawnLocation`. Used by the DB flush loop in `FakePlayerManager` and anywhere a fresh location is needed without relying on stored state. - `FakePlayerManager.restorationInProgress` вҖ” boolean flag (with `isRestorationInProgress()` / `setRestorationInProgress()`) that is `true` during bot persistence restore and cleared after LP prefix refresh completes. Check this before triggering actions that should not run while bots are being restored.
4) Common tasks & minimal steps (how to implement typical changes) - Add a new command: - Create `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/command/MyCommand.java` implementing the command interface used by `CommandManager`. - Register it in `FakePlayerPlugin.onEnable()` with `commandManager.register(new MyCommand(...))`. - No manual help update is needed вҖ” `HelpCommand` reads live from `CommandManager`. - Ensure `plugin.yml` contains the `fpp` command or sub-command docs (command parsing done by `CommandManager`). - Add a config option: - Add default to `src/main/resources/config.yml`. - If needed, update `Config` accessor and `ConfigMigrator` to preserve old configs. - New options should be validated in `util/ConfigValidator.java` if they can be misconfigured. - Add a language message: - Add the key and default text to `src/main/resources/language/en.yml`. - Access it in code with `Lang.get("your-key")` or `Lang.raw("your-key", "placeholder", "value")`. - The file is hot-reloadable вҖ” `/fpp reload` picks up changes without restart. - Add a permission node: - Declare a new `public static final String` constant in `permission/Perm.java` (e.g. `public static final String MY_FEATURE = "fpp.myfeature";`). - Add a full entry under `permissions:` in `plugin.yml` with description and default value (e.g. `op`, `true`, or `false`). - Add the node to the `fpp.*` wildcard's `children:` list in `plugin.yml` (so admins inherit it automatically). - Use the constant in your command's `getPermission()` and `canUse()` methods: `Perm.has(sender, Perm.MY_FEATURE)`. - **Critical:** LuckPerms reads `plugin.yml` at startup for tab-completion вҖ” permissions missing from `plugin.yml` won't appear in `/lp permission set fpp.<tab>`. - Test: restart server, run `/lp user <name> permission set fpp.` and verify your new permission appears in tab-completion.
- If changing skin behaviour or adding skins: ensure `SkinRepository`/`SkinFetcher` are respected вҖ” add skin files to `src/main/resources/skins/` for defaults or `plugins/FakePlayerPlugin/skins/` at runtime and call `/fpp reload` to refresh. The startup code also drops a `skins/README.txt` inside the plugin data folder on first run. **Note (v1.5.4):** `skin.guaranteed-skin` default changed from `true` вҶ’ `false`; bots with non-Mojang names now use Steve/Alex skins by default. `skin.fallback-pool` (`Config.skinFallbackPool()`) and `skin.fallback-name` (`Config.skinFallbackName()`) still exist as auto-mode fallbacks for guaranteed-skin resolution.
5) Build / run / verify (Windows PowerShell) Build, obfuscate, AND auto-deploy to `%USERPROFILE%\Desktop\dmc\plugins\fpp.jar` in one command: ```powershell mvn -DskipTests clean package ``` The `maven-antrun-plugin` in `pom.xml` automatically copies `target/fpp-*-obfuscated.jar` to the deploy folder after ProGuard finishes. To deploy to a different path override the property: ```powershell mvn -DskipTests clean package "-Ddeploy.dir=C:\path\to\server\plugins" ``` Note: requires JDK 21+ to compile (`pom.xml` `<java.version>21`). FastStats jars are bundled as binary resources in `src/main/resources/faststats/` (not shaded).
**IntelliJ build tool ("Build Artifacts"):** IntelliJ's native artifact builder bypasses Maven and ProGuard. Treat `target/fpp-*-obfuscated.jar` as the source artifact and use `pom.xml` as the source of truth for the current version; some repo docs/scripts still mention `1.4.22`. Workflow: 1. Run the **"Build Plugin (Obfuscated)"** Maven run configuration (or `mvn clean package`) вҖ” this runs ProGuard and auto-deploys 2. Optionally run **Build вҖә Build Artifacts** to re-pack the already-obfuscated JAR to the output path without recompiling - вҡ пёҸ "Build Artifacts" alone (without Maven running first) will fail if `target/fpp-*-obfuscated.jar` does not exist
Double-click `BUILD.bat` in the project root as an alternative to Maven in terminal.
For the optional Node/Vercel sidecar (`frontend/`), use the root `package.json` scripts: `npm install`, then `npm start` (local Express server via `frontend/index.js`) or `npm run dev`.
Start server (attach debugger): ```powershell java -agentlib:jdwp=transport=dt_socket,server=y,suspend=n,address=*:5005 -Xms512M -Xmx2G -jar .\paper-1.21.11.jar nogui ``` Tail logs: ```powershell Get-Content .\logs\latest.log -Wait ``` In-server quick checks: `/fpp list`, `/fpp spawn`, `/fpp reload` and watch console for startup banner messages printed by `FppLogger`.
6) Debugging tips - To reproduce startup ordering bugs, inspect `onEnable()` in `FakePlayerPlugin.java` вҖ” migration вҶ’ config вҶ’ Lang вҶ’ **version gate** (`CompatibilityChecker.isVersionAtLeast(detectedMcVersion, "1.21.12")` вҖ” sets `versionUnsupported`; runs between Lang and BotNameConfig) вҶ’ BotNameConfig/BotMessageConfig вҶ’ ensureDataDirectories вҶ’ SkinRepository.init вҶ’ database вҶ’ **BotIdentityCache** вҶ’ remote bot cache (NETWORK mode: pre-populate from DB) вҶ’ ConfigSyncManager (NETWORK mode only) вҶ’ FakePlayerManager+subsystems вҶ’ commands (**SettingGui instantiated here; `SettingCommand` registered; `settingGui` registered as listener immediately after**) вҶ’ listeners вҶ’ **BotChatAI + BotSwapAI + PeakHoursManager init** (PeakHoursManager started if `peak-hours.enabled && swap.enabled`) вҶ’ VelocityChannel (plugin messaging) вҶ’ periodic health check (6000 ticks) вҶ’ ConfigValidator вҶ’ TabListManager вҶ’ BotTabTeam вҶ’ PlaceholderAPI вҶ’ LuckPerms (`subscribeLpEvents` вҖ” direct, not deferred) вҶ’ **WorldGuard** (sets `worldGuardAvailable`) вҶ’ UpdateChecker (purely async, never blocks startup) вҶ’ Metrics вҶ’ startup banner вҶ’ persistence restore вҶ’ **PeakHoursManager crash recovery** (`restoreSleepingBotsFromDatabase` вҖ” only when DB enabled and `persist-on-restart: true`). Note: `CompatibilityChecker.check()` is no longer called at startup вҖ” all features are enabled unconditionally. - To debug config migrations, read `util/ConfigMigrator.java` and check backups under `plugins/FakePlayerPlugin/backups/` created by `BackupManager`. - For packet-level issues (spawn / entity problems) inspect `fakeplayer/PacketHelper.java` and `fakeplayer/FakePlayerManager.java`. - For database issues: SQLite file is at `plugins/FakePlayerPlugin/data/fpp.db`; run `/fpp migrate status` to check schema version, YAML sync health, and backup count. Full `/fpp migrate` subcommands: `backup` (create manual backup), `backups` (list stored backups), `status` (config version + file sync health + DB stats), `config` (re-run config migration chain), `lang` / `names` / `messages` (force-sync the respective YAML file from JAR), `db merge [file]` / `db export` / `db tomysql`. Set `database.enabled: false` in config to disable all DB I/O. - Tab list header/footer and update checker: `TabListManager.start()` and `UpdateChecker.check(this)` are run during `onEnable()` вҖ” useful when diagnosing missing tab headers or unexpected update-checker output.
7) Integration & soft-dependencies - PlaceholderAPI: `util.FppPlaceholderExpansion` registered if plugin present. **Full placeholder list (29+ total)** вҖ” server-wide: `%fpp_count%` (bot count вҖ” local + remote in NETWORK mode), `%fpp_local_count%` (this server's bots only), `%fpp_network_count%` (remote servers' bots only), `%fpp_max%` (global cap, `вҲһ` if 0), `%fpp_real%` (non-bot players online), `%fpp_total%` (real + bots, network-wide), `%fpp_online%` (alias for `%fpp_total%`), `%fpp_frozen%`, `%fpp_names%` (comma-joined display names вҖ” local + remote in NETWORK mode), `%fpp_network_names%` (remote-server bot names only), `%fpp_chat%`, `%fpp_swap%`, `%fpp_skin%`, `%fpp_body%`, `%fpp_pushable%`, `%fpp_damageable%`, `%fpp_tab%`, `%fpp_max_health%`, `%fpp_persistence%`, `%fpp_version%`. Network/Proxy: `%fpp_network%` (on/off for NETWORK mode), `%fpp_server_id%`, `%fpp_spawn_cooldown%`. Per-world: `%fpp_count_<world>%`, `%fpp_real_<world>%`, `%fpp_total_<world>%`. Player-relative: `%fpp_user_count%` (bots owned by that player), `%fpp_user_max%` (personal limit), `%fpp_user_names%` (comma-joined names of that player's bots). `%fpp_real%` = `Bukkit.getOnlinePlayers().size() - localBots` вҖ” bots go through `placeNewPlayer()` so they appear in `getOnlinePlayers()`; FPP subtracts local bot count to get the true real-player count. `%fpp_total%` = `real + bots`. - LuckPerms: `LuckPermsHelper.subscribeLpEvents(plugin, manager)` is called directly in `onEnable` (not deferred) for auto-prefix/suffix refresh via `UserDataRecalculateEvent`. - WorldGuard: soft-depend declared in `plugin.yml`. `WorldGuardHelper.isPvpAllowed(location)` cancels player-sourced PvP damage to bots in no-PvP regions (handled in `FakePlayerEntityListener` at `LOWEST` priority). `worldGuardAvailable` is cached in `onEnable` via a pure Bukkit plugin check вҖ” **never call `WorldGuardHelper` directly without first checking `plugin.isWorldGuardAvailable()`** (same ClassLoader guard as LuckPerms). - Vercel / Node API: `UpdateChecker` falls back from Modrinth to `https://fake-player-plugin.vercel.app`; `frontend/api/check-update.js` and `frontend/api/status.js` intentionally emit Java-compatible keys such as `remoteVersion`, `version`, `version_number`, and `downloadUrl`. - Metrics: `util/FppMetrics` wraps FastStats and is conditional via `config.yml` flag (`Config.metricsEnabled()`).
8) Useful repo search queries (quick semantic/grep targets) - "onEnable(" вҖ” find startup flow - "Config.init(" вҖ” find config usage - "getCommand(\"fpp\")" вҖ” command wiring - "registerEvents(" вҖ” listener registration - "purgeOrphanedBodiesAndRestore(" вҖ” persistence restore - "spawnFakePlayer(" or "PacketHelper" вҖ” low-level spawn - "Lang.get(" вҖ” message rendering pattern - "Perm." вҖ” permission check usages - "FakePlayerBody.spawn" вҖ” entity spawning entry point - "implements FppCommand" вҖ” all sub-command implementations - "DatabaseManager" вҖ” database call sites - "YamlFileSyncer.syncMissingKeys" вҖ” YAML auto-sync call sites (Lang, BotNameConfig, BotMessageConfig) - "isOnCooldown" вҖ” spawn cooldown enforcement - "updateBotPrefix(" or "refreshLpDisplayName(" вҖ” single-bot LP prefix refresh (called by finishSpawn and RankCommand) - "implements FppCommand" or "RankCommand" вҖ” find rank/LP group assignment logic - "VelocityChannel" вҖ” proxy plugin-messaging send/receive - "RemoteBotCache" вҖ” remote bot tab-list entries from other servers - "isNetworkMode" вҖ” NETWORK mode guard sites - "ConfigSyncManager" вҖ” config sync push/pull - "BotPvpAI" or "pvpAI.tickBot" вҖ” PVP bot AI tick loop and state machine - "BotType.PVP" or "getBotType()" вҖ” PVP bot type checks and spawn guards - "InventoryCommand" or "openGui" or "invToBot" вҖ” bot inventory GUI implementation - "MoveCommand" or "startNavigation" or "BotPathfinder" вҖ” bot pathfinding / /fpp move - "requestNavJump" or "clearNavJump" or "navJumpHolding" вҖ” navigation jump coordination in FakePlayerManager - "XpCommand" or "isOnXpCooldown" or "BotXpPickupListener" вҖ” XP collection command and pickup gating - "setChatEnabled" or "setChatTier" or "timedMute" вҖ” per-bot chat control - "BotCombatMode" вҖ” crystal/sword/fist combat mode detection - "PeakHoursManager" or "peakHoursManager" вҖ” time-window bot pool scheduler - "putBotToSleepByName" or "wakeBotByName" вҖ” manual peak-hours sleep/wake - "restoreSleepingBotsFromDatabase" вҖ” peak-hours crash recovery on startup - "SettingGui" or "applyLiveEffect" вҖ” in-game settings GUI and live config-key effect application - "BotIdentityCache" or "resolveUuid" or "identityCache" вҖ” stable nameвҶ’UUID identity system (prevents bots from appearing as new players on re-spawn) - "lookupBotUuid" or "registerBotUuid" вҖ” DatabaseManager identity API called by BotIdentityCache - "bot-identities.yml" вҖ” YAML fallback for identity persistence when database is disabled - "broadcastBotDisplayNameUpdate" вҖ” VelocityChannel BOT_UPDATE subchannel for display name sync - "WorldGuardHelper" or "isWorldGuardAvailable" вҖ” WorldGuard PvP region protection integration - "isPvpAllowed" вҖ” WorldGuard PvP flag query in FakePlayerEntityListener - "versionUnsupported" or "isVersionUnsupported" вҖ” MC version gate (blocks all /fpp sub-commands on MC 1.21.12+) - "stampFirstPlayed" or "forceHasPlayedBefore" вҖ” PlayerJoinListener reflection helpers for management plugin compat
9) Where to read docs - `docs/` вҖ” internal build/setup/release notes (`BUILD_INSTRUCTIONS.md`, `INTELLIJ_BUILD_SETUP.md`, `OBFUSCATION_CORRECTED.md`, `PROJECT_ORGANIZATION.md`, `RELEASE-1.4.23.md`, `RELEASE-1.5.8.md`, `SKIN-SYSTEM-FIX.md`, `BOT-SYSTEM-IMPLEMENTATION-STATUS.md`, `BOT-SYSTEM-IMPROVEMENTS.md`, `FIX-AI-STATE-CLEANUP.md`); current plugin version is `1.5.17`, current `config-version` stamped by `ConfigMigrator.CURRENT_VERSION` is `51` вҖ” always use `pom.xml` as the source of truth because some repo docs/scripts still mention `1.4.22`. Workflow: - `wiki/` вҖ” developer-facing docs (Commands.md, Configuration.md, Getting-Started.md, Database.md, Language.md, Permissions.md, Skin-System.md, Swap-System.md, Bot-Behaviour.md, Bot-Messages.md, Bot-Names.md, Fake-Chat.md, FAQ.md, Migration.md, Home.md, Placeholders.md, Proxy-Support.md, Config-Sync.md, **Peak-Hours.md**) - `README.md` вҖ” general usage and release notes
Contact / notes - This file focuses on discoverable, actionable patterns only. For design rationale, see `wiki/Migration.md` and issues in the project tracker.

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
@echo off setlocal title FakePlayerPlugin Builder color 0A
echo. echo =============================================== echo FakePlayerPlugin Build Script echo =============================================== echo. echo [INFO] Starting build process... echo [INFO] This will compile, obfuscate, and deploy the plugin echo.
REM Change to project root (one level up from build/) cd /d "%~dp0.."
REM Check if Maven is available where mvn >nul 2>&1 if %errorlevel% neq 0 ( echo [ERROR] Maven (mvn) not found in PATH echo [ERROR] Please install Maven and add it to your PATH echo. pause exit /b 1 )
REM Run the Maven build echo [INFO] Running: mvn -DskipTests clean package echo. call mvn -DskipTests clean package set BUILD_EXIT=%errorlevel%
if %BUILD_EXIT% equ 0 ( echo. echo =============================================== echo BUILD SUCCESSFUL! echo =============================================== echo. for %%F in ("target\fpp-*-obfuscated.jar") do ( echo [SUCCESS] Obfuscated JAR: %%F echo [INFO] File size: %%~zF bytes ) echo. echo [BUILD] Copied to build\fpp.jar ^(project root^) echo [DEPLOY] Copied to %USERPROFILE%\Desktop\dmc\plugins\fpp.jar echo. echo [TIP] To deploy to a different server folder, run: echo mvn -DskipTests clean package -Ddeploy.dir=C:\path\to\server\plugins ) else ( echo. echo =============================================== echo BUILD FAILED! echo =============================================== echo. echo [ERROR] Build failed with exit code %BUILD_EXIT% echo [ERROR] Check the Maven output above for details )
echo. echo Press any key to close this window... pause >nul endlocal

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
@echo off setlocal title FakePlayerPlugin Builder color 0A
echo. echo =============================================== echo FakePlayerPlugin ^| BUILD echo =============================================== echo.
REM Change to project root (one level up from build/) cd /d "%~dp0.."
REM Set JAVA_HOME (JDK 25 preferred; pom.xml handles ProGuard JDK selection internally) set "JDK25=C:\Program Files\Java\jdk-25.0.2" set "JDK21=C:\Program Files\Java\jdk-21"
if exist "%JDK25%\bin\java.exe" ( set "JAVA_HOME=%JDK25%" set "PATH=%JDK25%\bin;%PATH%" echo [INFO] Using JDK 25: %JDK25% ) else if exist "%JDK21%\bin\java.exe" ( set "JAVA_HOME=%JDK21%" set "PATH=%JDK21%\bin;%PATH%" echo [INFO] Using JDK 21: %JDK21% ) else ( echo [INFO] JAVA_HOME not overridden - using system default )
REM Check if Maven is available where mvn >nul 2>&1 if %errorlevel% neq 0 ( echo. echo [ERROR] Maven ^(mvn^) not found in PATH. echo [ERROR] Please install Maven and add it to your PATH, then retry. echo. pause exit /b 1 )
echo. echo [INFO] Running: mvn -DskipTests clean package echo. call mvn -DskipTests clean package set BUILD_EXIT=%errorlevel%
echo. if %BUILD_EXIT% equ 0 goto :success goto :failed
:success echo =============================================== echo BUILD SUCCESSFUL! echo =============================================== echo. for %%F in ("target\fpp-*-obfuscated.jar") do ( echo [OUTPUT] %%F echo [SIZE] %%~zF bytes ) if exist "target\mapping.txt" ( for %%M in ("target\mapping.txt") do ( echo [MAPPING] target\mapping.txt ^(%%~zM bytes^) echo Keep this file to decode obfuscated stack traces. ) ) echo. echo [BUILD] Copied to build\fpp.jar ^(project root^) echo [DEPLOY] Copied to %USERPROFILE%\Desktop\dmc\plugins\fpp.jar echo. echo Tips: echo Deploy elsewhere : mvn -DskipTests clean package -Ddeploy.dir=C:\path\to\plugins echo Skip obfuscation : mvn -DskipTests clean package -Dskip.proguard=true goto :done
:failed echo =============================================== echo BUILD FAILED! echo =============================================== echo. echo [ERROR] Exit code: %BUILD_EXIT% echo [ERROR] Check the Maven output above for details. echo. echo Common causes: echo Compile error - fix Java source errors first echo Missing dependency - run: mvn dependency:resolve echo ProGuard crash - see pom.xml proguard-obfuscation exec config
:done echo. echo Press any key to close this window... pause >nul endlocal

option.name: filePath
option.value: /Bot-Behaviour (snapshot).md

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
# Bot Behaviour
> **Understanding Bot Physics, AI, and Interactions** > **Complete Guide to Mannequin Entities and Bot Intelligence**
---
## Overview
FPP bots are sophisticated entities that combine **realistic tab list presence** with **physical world interaction**. Each bot consists of multiple components working together to create a seamless fake player experience.
### пёҸ **Bot Architecture**
``` Fake Player (Bot) в”ңв”Җв”Җ Tab List Entry # Shows in player list в”ңв”Җв”Җ Chat Presence # Can send messages в”ңв”Җв”Җ Physical Body # Mannequin entity (optional) в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ Skin Display # Visual appearance в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ Head AI # Head rotation tracking в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ Combat System # Damage and death в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җ Physics # Movement and collision в””в”Җв”Җ AI Systems # Behavior and intelligence в”ңв”Җв”Җ Chat AI # Message broadcasting в”ңв”Җв”Җ Swap AI # Player replacement в””в”Җв”Җ Chunk Loading # World presence ```
---
## пёҸ **Bot Protection System** *(v1.5.6+)*
FPP includes automatic protection systems to prevent common issues with plugin interference and bot abuse.
### **Command Blocking**
**Automatic 4-Layer Protection**
Bots are **completely command-proof** вҖ” they cannot execute commands from any source:
``` Layer 1: LOWEST Priority вҶ’ Catch commands first Layer 2: HIGHEST Priority вҶ’ Safety net for edge cases Layer 3: MONITOR Priority вҶ’ Final safeguard, re-cancel if needed Layer 4: CommandSend Event вҶ’ Clear command suggestions ```
**Protected Against:** - вң… First-join command plugins (e.g., giving starter kits to bots) - вң… Auto-command schedulers running commands on bots - вң… Permission-based command executors - вң… `Player.performCommand()` calls from other plugins - вң… `Bukkit.dispatchCommand()` calls - вң… Command suggestions and tab-completion
**Works Automatically:** - вқҢ No configuration needed - вқҢ No permissions needed - вң… Completely transparent - вң… Zero performance impact
**Debug Logging:**
```yaml logging: debug: nms: true # Shows command blocking in action ```
**Console Output:** ``` [FPP] BotCommandBlocker: blocked command (LOWEST) for Bot123: /give Bot123 diamond_sword [FPP] BotCommandBlocker: cleared command suggestions for Bot123 ```
---
### **Lobby Spawn Protection**
**5-Tick Grace Period**
Bots are **protected from lobby plugin teleports** during their initial spawn (250ms):
**What It Blocks:** - вң… EssentialsX spawn-on-join - вң… Multiverse respawn anchors - вң… Custom lobby teleport plugins - вң… Any `PlayerTeleportEvent` with PLUGIN or UNKNOWN cause
**What It Allows:** - вң… Admin commands (`/tp`, `/fpp tp`, `/fpp tph`) - вң… Manual teleports - вң… Teleports after the grace period expires
**How It Works:**
``` 1. Bot spawns at player's location 2. Protection activated (5 ticks / 250ms) 3. Lobby plugins' teleport attempts are blocked 4. Protection expires after 5 ticks 5. Bot remains at correct location ```
**Debug Logging:**
```yaml logging: debug: nms: true # Shows spawn protection in action ```
**Console Output:** ``` [FPP] BotSpawnProtection: protecting Bot123 from teleports for 5 ticks [FPP] BotSpawnProtection: blocked PLUGIN teleport for Bot123 from world (100,64,200) to lobby (0,100,0) [FPP] BotSpawnProtection: removed protection for Bot123 ```
---
### **Physical Bodies**
Each bot is a full **NMS ServerPlayer** entity вҖ” not a Mannequin. It has a real player model, hitbox, and physics.
### вҡҷпёҸ **Body Configuration**
```yaml body: enabled: true # Spawn a visible entity in the world pushable: true # Players can push bots damageable: true # Bots take damage and can die ```
Both `pushable` and `damageable` are **live-reloadable** via `/fpp reload`.
**Head AI:** ```yaml head-ai: enabled: true look-range: 8.0 # Detection radius in blocks turn-speed: 0.3 # 0.0 = frozen, 1.0 = instant snap ```
**Swim AI:** ```yaml swim-ai: enabled: true # Bots swim upward in water/lava; false = bots sink ```
When `swim-ai.enabled: true`, bots automatically hold jump while submerged вҖ” mimicking a real player pressing spacebar in water or lava.
### **Visual Appearance**
- Skins from the [Skin System](Skin-System.md) - Display name in configurable format with LuckPerms prefix/suffix - Full color code and MiniMessage formatting support
---
## **Head AI System**
### **Intelligent Head Tracking**
Bots smoothly rotate to look at the nearest player within a configurable range.
**Configuration (config.yml):** ```yaml head-ai: enabled: true look-range: 8.0 # Detection radius in blocks turn-speed: 0.3 # Rotation smoothing (0.0 = frozen, 1.0 = instant snap) ```
### **Tracking Behavior**
**Target Priority:** 1. **Closest player** within range 2. **Smooth rotation** вҖ” no instant snapping; `turn-speed` controls how fast the head turns each tick
**Visual Effect:** - Bot head follows nearby players naturally - Creates an impression of awareness and engagement - Set `look-range: 0` to disable head tracking entirely
---
## **Combat & Damage System**
### вҡ”пёҸ **Damage Mechanics**
**Damage Configuration (config.yml):** ```yaml body: damageable: true # Bots can take damage (hot-reloadable)
combat: max-health: 20.0 # Health points (20.0 = standard player health) hurt-sound: true # Play hurt sound when bot takes damage ```
### **Death System**
**When a Bot Dies:** 1. **Death Animation** вҖ” Plays death animation and sound 2. **Entity Cleanup** вҖ” Bot entity is removed from the world 3. **Tab List Update** вҖ” Bot disappears from the player list 4. **Event Logging** вҖ” Records death in database (if enabled)
**Death Sources:** - вң… Player attacks (sword, bow, etc.) - вң… Environmental damage (lava, fall damage, drowning) - вң… Entity damage (monsters, explosions) - вқҢ Plugin damage (when `damageable: false`)
**Respawn Options:** ```yaml death: respawn-on-death: false # Respawn bot instead of removing it on death respawn-delay: 60 # Ticks before respawn (60 = 3 seconds) suppress-drops: true # Prevent item drops on death ```
---
## **Physics & Collision**
### **Movement Physics**
Bots support **realistic physics simulation** including collision, pushing, and movement.
**Push Configuration (config.yml):** ```yaml bot-collision: enabled: true # Enable collision physics
# Player вҶ’ Bot collision walk-radius: 1.5 # Activation distance walk-strength: 0.3 # Push force multiplier
# Player hits Bot collision hit-strength: 0.8 # Punch force multiplier
# Bot вҶ’ Bot collision bot-radius: 1.0 # Bot separation distance bot-strength: 0.2 # Inter-bot push force
# Movement limits max-horizontal-speed: 2.0 # Maximum push speed vertical-damping: 0.1 # Reduce vertical movement ```
### **Collision Types**
**1. Player Walks Into Bot** - **Trigger:** Player gets within `walk-radius` - **Effect:** Bot is gently pushed aside with `walk-strength` - **Use Case:** Natural movement around bots
**2. Player Hits Bot** - **Trigger:** Player attacks bot (punch, weapon) - **Effect:** Stronger push with `hit-strength` - **Use Case:** Intentional bot positioning
**3. Bot Bumps Bot** - **Trigger:** Two bots get within `bot-radius` of each other - **Effect:** Bots push apart with `bot-strength` - **Use Case:** Prevents bot clustering
### вҡЎ **Performance Optimization**
**Physics Settings:** ```yaml bot-collision: update-interval: 5 # Physics update frequency (ticks) max-entities-per-tick: 10 # Limit collision calculations enable-optimization: true # Use spatial optimization ```
**Optimization Features:** - **Spatial partitioning** вҖ” Only check nearby entities - **Update throttling** вҖ” Limit calculations per tick - **Distance culling** вҖ” Skip distant collision checks - **Smart activation** вҖ” Only process when needed
---
## **Chunk Loading System**
### **World Presence**
Bots can **keep chunks loaded** around their location to maintain world presence even when no players are nearby.
**Configuration (config.yml):** ```yaml chunk-loading: enabled: true # Enable chunk loading radius: 2 # Chunks to keep loaded (radius) unload-delay: 300 # Delay before unloading (seconds) max-loaded-chunks: 50 # Global limit on bot-loaded chunks ```
### **Chunk Management**
**Loading Behavior:** - **Load on spawn** вҖ” Bot loads surrounding chunks when created - **Maintain presence** вҖ” Keeps chunks loaded while bot exists - **Smart unloading** вҖ” Releases chunks when bot is removed - **Memory management** вҖ” Respects server chunk limits
**Radius Examples:** - `radius: 1` вҖ” 9 chunks (3x3 area) - `radius: 2` вҖ” 25 chunks (5x5 area) - `radius: 3` вҖ” 49 chunks (7x7 area)
**Performance Impact:** ```yaml chunk-loading: enabled: false # Disable if causing lag radius: 1 # Minimize radius for performance ```
---
## **Chat AI System**
### пёҸ **Intelligent Messaging**
Bots can **automatically send chat messages** to create realistic server activity and engagement.
**Basic Configuration (config.yml):** ```yaml fake-chat: enabled: true # Enable fake chat interval: 300-900 # Message frequency (seconds) chat-format: "&7{prefix}{bot_name}{suffix}: {message}" ```
### **Message System**
**Message Pool (bot-messages.yml):** ```yaml messages: general: - "Hello everyone!" - "How's everyone doing?" - "Nice weather today"
greetings: - "Good morning!" - "Hey there!"
questions: - "Anyone want to build something?" - "What's everyone up to?" ```
**Chat Features:** - **Random selection** вҖ” Messages chosen from pools randomly - **Timing variation** вҖ” Interval ranges prevent predictable patterns - **LuckPerms integration** вҖ” Prefixes and suffixes in messages - **Color support** вҖ” Full MiniMessage and legacy color codes
### **Advanced Chat Behavior**
**Smart Messaging:** ```yaml fake-chat: player-requirement: 2 # Only chat when 2+ real players online cooldown: 60 # Minimum delay between bot messages max-per-hour: 10 # Limit messages per bot per hour ```
**Contextual Messages:** - **Time-based** вҖ” Different messages for day/night - **Event-triggered** вҖ” Messages on player join/leave - **Location-aware** вҖ” Different messages per world
---
## **Swap AI System**
### **Player Replacement**
The **Swap System** can **automatically replace offline players** with bots to maintain consistent server population.
**Configuration (config.yml):** ```yaml swap-system: enabled: true # Enable player swapping delay: 300 # Seconds before swapping offline player probability: 0.7 # Chance to swap (70%) max-swaps: 10 # Maximum simultaneous swaps ```
### **Swap Intelligence**
**Replacement Logic:** 1. **Monitor departures** вҖ” Track when players leave 2. **Wait period** вҖ” Delay before considering swap 3. **Eligibility check** вҖ” Verify player can be swapped 4. **Bot creation** вҖ” Spawn bot with similar characteristics 5. **Seamless transition** вҖ” Maintain server activity appearance
**Smart Features:** - **Whitelist protection** вҖ” VIP players never swapped - **Permission respect** вҖ” Honors bypass permissions - **Natural behavior** вҖ” Bots act like the replaced player - **Automatic cleanup** вҖ” Removes bots when player returns
### **Swap Targeting**
**Player Selection:** ```yaml swap-system: min-playtime: 1800 # Minimum seconds online before swapping whitelist-enabled: true # Use whitelist protection respect-permissions: true # Check swap bypass permissions ```
**Protected Players:** - Players with `fpp.bypass.swap` permission - Players in configured whitelist - Players online less than minimum time - Staff members (configurable)
---
## **Performance & Optimization**
### вҡЎ **System Performance**
**Bot Limits:** ```yaml global-bot-limit: 50 # Total bots on server performance: max-ai-updates: 20 # AI updates per tick physics-interval: 5 # Physics update frequency cleanup-interval: 1200 # Cleanup frequency (ticks) ```
### **Optimization Strategies**
**1. AI Optimization:** - **Update throttling** вҖ” Spread AI calculations across ticks - **Distance culling** вҖ” Disable AI when no players nearby - **Smart activation** вҖ” Only run needed AI systems - **Batch processing** вҖ” Group similar calculations
**2. Physics Optimization:** - **Spatial indexing** вҖ” Efficient collision detection - **Update limiting** вҖ” Control physics calculation frequency - **LOD system** вҖ” Reduce detail for distant bots - **Memory pooling** вҖ” Reuse calculation objects
**3. Network Optimization:** - **Packet batching** вҖ” Group network updates - **Update filtering** вҖ” Send only necessary changes - **Compression** вҖ” Optimize packet size - **Rate limiting** вҖ” Control update frequency
### **Monitoring Performance**
**Built-in Diagnostics:** ```bash /fpp stats --detailed # Comprehensive bot statistics /timings report # Server performance analysis ```
**Performance Metrics:** - **Bot count** вҖ” Total active bots - **AI load** вҖ” Processing time per tick - **Physics load** вҖ” Collision calculation time - **Memory usage** вҖ” RAM consumed by bot systems - **Network usage** вҖ” Packets sent for bot updates
---
## пёҸ **Advanced Configuration**
### **Compatibility Mode**
For servers with limited Mannequin support:
```yaml compatibility: restricted-mode: true # Auto-detected disable-bodies: true # Force disable physical bodies disable-physics: true # Disable collision system disable-head-ai: true # Disable head rotation ```
**Restricted Mode Effects:** - **Tab-only bots** вҖ” Bots appear in tab list only - **No physical presence** вҖ” No entities in world - **Chat functionality** вҖ” Fake chat still works - **Swap system** вҖ” Player replacement still available
### **Advanced Physics**
**Detailed Physics Control:** ```yaml bot-collision: # Advanced collision settings friction: 0.8 # Surface friction coefficient restitution: 0.1 # Bounce factor gravity-factor: 1.0 # Gravity strength multiplier
# Collision layers player-layer: true # Collision with players entity-layer: false # Collision with mobs block-layer: true # Collision with blocks
# Performance tuning spatial-hash-size: 16 # Spatial optimization grid size max-iterations: 10 # Physics solver iterations ```
### **AI Behavior Tuning**
**Head AI Advanced Settings:** ```yaml bot-head-ai: # Targeting behavior player-priority: true # Prefer players over other targets movement-attraction: 1.5 # Attraction to moving targets look-attraction: 2.0 # Attraction when looked at
# Rotation mechanics smoothing: 0.8 # Rotation smoothing factor prediction: true # Predict player movement max-angle: 90 # Maximum head rotation (degrees)
# Performance update-distance: 32 # Maximum tracking distance cpu-budget: 0.5 # Milliseconds per tick budget ```
---
## **Use Cases & Examples**
### **Server Population Management**
**Scenario:** Maintain active appearance during off-peak hours ```yaml # Configuration for population bots body: spawn-body: true # Visible presence in world pushable: false # Prevent griefing damageable: false # Immortal population bots
fake-chat: enabled: true # Create chat activity interval: 600-1800 # Moderate message frequency
swap-system: enabled: true # Replace offline players delay: 180 # Quick replacement ```
### **Minigame NPCs**
**Scenario:** Create interactive NPCs for game lobbies ```yaml # NPC-like behavior bot-head-ai: enabled: true # Acknowledge players range: 5.0 # Close interaction range
body: pushable: false # Static positioning damageable: false # Immortal NPCs
chunk-loading: enabled: true # Always loaded radius: 1 # Minimal impact ```
### **Roleplay Characters**
**Scenario:** Populate towns with realistic inhabitants ```yaml # Realistic town folk fake-chat: enabled: true # Roleplay conversations chat-format: "&f{prefix}{bot_name}: {message}"
bot-collision: enabled: true # Natural movement walk-strength: 0.1 # Gentle displacement
bot-head-ai: enabled: true # Social awareness range: 6.0 # Natural interaction range ```
---
## **Troubleshooting**
### вқҢ **Common Issues**
**Bots Not Moving/Rotating:** - вң… Check `bot-head-ai.enabled: true` - вң… Verify `body.spawn-body: true` - вң… Ensure players are within range - вң… Check compatibility mode status
**Performance Problems:** - вң… Reduce bot limits in config - вң… Disable unnecessary AI systems - вң… Increase update intervals - вң… Monitor with `/fpp stats --detailed`
**Physics Not Working:** - вң… Verify `bot-collision.enabled: true` - вң… Check `body.pushable: true` - вң… Ensure physical bodies are enabled - вң… Test collision ranges and strengths
### пёҸ **Diagnostic Commands**
```bash /fpp stats --detailed # Performance and behavior stats /fpp info <botname> # Individual bot status /fpp freeze <botname> # Test bot responsiveness /fpp list --frozen # Check frozen bot status ```
---
## **Related Documentation**
- **[Skin System](Skin-System.md)** вҖ” Bot visual appearance - **[Fake Chat](Fake-Chat.md)** вҖ” Chat AI configuration - **[Swap System](Swap-System.md)** вҖ” Player replacement details - **[Configuration](Configuration.md)** вҖ” All behavior settings - **[Performance Guide](FAQ.md#performance-issues)** вҖ” Optimization tips
---
** Master bot behavior to create the most realistic and engaging fake players!**

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Bot Behaviour
> **Understanding Bot Physics, AI, and Interactions** > **Complete Guide to Mannequin Entities and Bot Intelligence**
---
## Overview
FPP bots are sophisticated entities that combine **realistic tab list presence** with **physical world interaction**. Each bot consists of multiple components working together to create a seamless fake player experience.
### пёҸ **Bot Architecture**
``` Fake Player (Bot) в”ңв”Җв”Җ Tab List Entry # Shows in player list в”ңв”Җв”Җ Chat Presence # Can send messages в”ңв”Җв”Җ Physical Body # Mannequin entity (optional) в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ Skin Display # Visual appearance в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ Head AI # Head rotation tracking в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ Combat System # Damage and death в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җ Physics # Movement and collision в””в”Җв”Җ AI Systems # Behavior and intelligence в”ңв”Җв”Җ Chat AI # Message broadcasting в”ңв”Җв”Җ Swap AI # Player replacement в””в”Җв”Җ Chunk Loading # World presence ```
---
## пёҸ **Bot Protection System** *(v1.5.6+)*
FPP includes automatic protection systems to prevent common issues with plugin interference and bot abuse.
### **Command Blocking**
**Automatic 4-Layer Protection**
Bots are **completely command-proof** вҖ” they cannot execute commands from any source:
``` Layer 1: LOWEST Priority вҶ’ Catch commands first Layer 2: HIGHEST Priority вҶ’ Safety net for edge cases Layer 3: MONITOR Priority вҶ’ Final safeguard, re-cancel if needed Layer 4: CommandSend Event вҶ’ Clear command suggestions ```
**Protected Against:** - вң… First-join command plugins (e.g., giving starter kits to bots) - вң… Auto-command schedulers running commands on bots - вң… Permission-based command executors - вң… `Player.performCommand()` calls from other plugins - вң… `Bukkit.dispatchCommand()` calls - вң… Command suggestions and tab-completion
**Works Automatically:** - вқҢ No configuration needed - вқҢ No permissions needed - вң… Completely transparent - вң… Zero performance impact
**Debug Logging:**
```yaml logging: debug: nms: true # Shows command blocking in action ```
**Console Output:** ``` [FPP] BotCommandBlocker: blocked command (LOWEST) for Bot123: /give Bot123 diamond_sword [FPP] BotCommandBlocker: cleared command suggestions for Bot123 ```
---
### **Lobby Spawn Protection**
**5-Tick Grace Period**
Bots are **protected from lobby plugin teleports** during their initial spawn (250ms):
**What It Blocks:** - вң… EssentialsX spawn-on-join - вң… Multiverse respawn anchors - вң… Custom lobby teleport plugins - вң… Any `PlayerTeleportEvent` with PLUGIN or UNKNOWN cause
**What It Allows:** - вң… Admin commands (`/tp`, `/fpp tp`, `/fpp tph`) - вң… Manual teleports - вң… Teleports after the grace period expires
**How It Works:**
``` 1. Bot spawns at player's location 2. Protection activated (5 ticks / 250ms) 3. Lobby plugins' teleport attempts are blocked 4. Protection expires after 5 ticks 5. Bot remains at correct location ```
**Debug Logging:**
```yaml logging: debug: nms: true # Shows spawn protection in action ```
**Console Output:** ``` [FPP] BotSpawnProtection: protecting Bot123 from teleports for 5 ticks [FPP] BotSpawnProtection: blocked PLUGIN teleport for Bot123 from world (100,64,200) to lobby (0,100,0) [FPP] BotSpawnProtection: removed protection for Bot123 ```
---
### **Physical Bodies**
Each bot is a full **NMS ServerPlayer** entity вҖ” not a Mannequin. It has a real player model, hitbox, and physics.
### вҡҷпёҸ **Body Configuration**
```yaml body: enabled: true # Spawn a visible entity in the world pushable: true # Players can push bots damageable: true # Bots take damage and can die ```
Both `pushable` and `damageable` are **live-reloadable** via `/fpp reload`.
**Head AI:** ```yaml head-ai: enabled: true look-range: 8.0 # Detection radius in blocks turn-speed: 0.3 # 0.0 = frozen, 1.0 = instant snap ```
**Swim AI:** ```yaml swim-ai: enabled: true # Bots swim upward in water/lava; false = bots sink ```
When `swim-ai.enabled: true`, bots automatically hold jump while submerged вҖ” mimicking a real player pressing spacebar in water or lava.
### **Visual Appearance**
- Skins from the [Skin System](Skin-System.md) - Display name in configurable format with LuckPerms prefix/suffix - Full color code and MiniMessage formatting support
---
## **Head AI System**
### **Intelligent Head Tracking**
Bots smoothly rotate to look at the nearest player within a configurable range.
**Configuration (config.yml):** ```yaml head-ai: enabled: true look-range: 8.0 # Detection radius in blocks turn-speed: 0.3 # Rotation smoothing (0.0 = frozen, 1.0 = instant snap) ```
### **Tracking Behavior**
**Target Priority:** 1. **Closest player** within range 2. **Smooth rotation** вҖ” no instant snapping; `turn-speed` controls how fast the head turns each tick
**Visual Effect:** - Bot head follows nearby players naturally - Creates an impression of awareness and engagement - Set `look-range: 0` to disable head tracking entirely
---
## **Combat & Damage System**
### вҡ”пёҸ **Damage Mechanics**
**Damage Configuration (config.yml):** ```yaml body: damageable: true # Bots can take damage (hot-reloadable)
combat: max-health: 20.0 # Health points (20.0 = standard player health) hurt-sound: true # Play hurt sound when bot takes damage ```
### **Death System**
**When a Bot Dies:** 1. **Death Animation** вҖ” Plays death animation and sound 2. **Entity Cleanup** вҖ” Bot entity is removed from the world 3. **Tab List Update** вҖ” Bot disappears from the player list 4. **Event Logging** вҖ” Records death in database (if enabled)
**Death Sources:** - вң… Player attacks (sword, bow, etc.) - вң… Environmental damage (lava, fall damage, drowning) - вң… Entity damage (monsters, explosions) - вқҢ Plugin damage (when `damageable: false`)
**Respawn Options:** ```yaml death: respawn-on-death: false # Respawn bot instead of removing it on death respawn-delay: 60 # Ticks before respawn (60 = 3 seconds) suppress-drops: true # Prevent item drops on death ```
---
## **Physics & Collision**
### **Movement Physics**
Bots support **realistic physics simulation** including collision, pushing, and movement.
**Push Configuration (config.yml):** ```yaml body: pushable: true # Allow players/entities to push bots (hot-reloadable)
collision: walk-radius: 0.85 # Player walk-into activation distance walk-strength: 0.22 # Push force when walking into a bot hit-strength: 0.45 # Knockback force when hitting a bot hit-max-horizontal-speed: 0.80 # Max horizontal speed for hit/explosion knockback bot-radius: 0.90 # Bot-vs-bot separation radius bot-strength: 0.14 # Separation force between bots max-horizontal-speed: 0.30 # Push speed cap for walk/separation sources ```
When `body.pushable: false`, all collision physics are disabled and bots become completely immovable.
### **Collision Types**
**1. Player Walks Into Bot** - **Trigger:** Player gets within `walk-radius` - **Effect:** Bot is gently pushed aside with `walk-strength`
**2. Player Hits Bot** - **Trigger:** Player attacks bot (punch, weapon) - **Effect:** Stronger push with `hit-strength`
**3. Bot Bumps Bot** - **Trigger:** Two bots get within `bot-radius` of each other - **Effect:** Bots push apart with `bot-strength` вҖ” prevents clustering
---
## **Chunk Loading System**
### **World Presence**
Bots keep chunks loaded around their location exactly like a real player вҖ” mobs spawn, redstone ticks, and crops grow inside the loaded radius.
**Configuration (config.yml):** ```yaml chunk-loading: enabled: true radius: 0 # 0 = match server simulation-distance update-interval: 20 # Ticks between position checks (20 = 1 s) ```
### **Chunk Management**
- **Load on spawn** вҖ” Bot loads surrounding chunks when created - **Maintain presence** вҖ” Keeps chunks loaded while bot exists - **Smart unloading** вҖ” Releases chunks when bot is removed
Set `chunk-loading.enabled: false` if chunk loading is causing performance issues.
---
## **Chat AI System**
### пёҸ **Intelligent Messaging**
Bots send random chat messages from `bot-messages.yml` at random intervals. See [Fake Chat](Fake-Chat.md) for full documentation.
**Basic Configuration (config.yml):** ```yaml fake-chat: enabled: false require-player-online: true chance: 0.75 interval: min: 5 max: 10 typing-delay: true # Simulate typing pause before messages activity-variation: true # Random per-bot chat frequency tier stagger-interval: 3 # Min seconds gap between any two bots chatting ```
**Chat Features:** - **Random selection** вҖ” Messages chosen from `bot-messages.yml` randomly - **Timing variation** вҖ” Per-bot independent intervals prevent predictable patterns - **LuckPerms integration** вҖ” `{prefix}` and `{suffix}` in `chat-format` - **Full color support** вҖ” MiniMessage and legacy `&` codes - **Event reactions** вҖ” Bots react to player joins, leaves, and deaths - **Mention replies** вҖ” Bots reply when a player says their name in chat
---
## **Swap AI System**
### **Session Rotation**
The Swap System periodically rotates bots вҖ” each bot leaves after a configurable session and rejoins with a fresh name. See [Swap System](Swap-System.md) for full documentation.
**Configuration (config.yml):** ```yaml swap: enabled: false session: min: 60 # Minimum session duration (seconds) max: 300 # Maximum session duration (seconds) absence: min: 30 # Minimum offline gap (seconds) max: 120 # Maximum offline gap (seconds) max-swapped-out: 0 # Max bots offline simultaneously (0 = unlimited) farewell-chat: true # Bot says goodbye before leaving greeting-chat: true # Bot says hi when rejoining same-name-on-rejoin: true # Reuse the same name if available ```
Toggle live with `/fpp swap` (bare command toggles on/off, just like `/fpp chat`).
### **Personality Archetypes**
Each bot gets a random personality multiplier when swap starts:
| Archetype | Session Modifier | |-----------|-----------------| | **quiet** | 2.0Г— вҖ” stays extended periods | | **passive** | 1.4Г— вҖ” below-average activity | | **normal** | 1.0Г— вҖ” typical session | | **active** | 0.7Г— вҖ” leaves more often | | **chatty** | 0.5Г— вҖ” quick popper |
---
## **Performance & Optimization**
### вҡЎ **System Performance**
**Bot Limits:** ```yaml limits: max-bots: 1000 # Global cap (0 = unlimited) ```
Monitor live with `/fpp stats` or `/fpp stats --detailed`.
### **Optimization Strategies**
1. **Start small** вҖ” Begin with 5вҖ“10 bots and increase gradually 2. **Disable unused systems** вҖ” Turn off chunk-loading, head-ai, or physics if not needed 3. **Tune intervals** вҖ” Increase `chunk-loading.update-interval` and `head-ai.turn-speed` thresholds 4. **Use `/fpp freeze`** вҖ” Freeze idle bots to stop their AI ticks 5. **Monitor TPS** вҖ” Use `/fpp stats` to watch for performance impact
---
## **Use Cases & Examples**
### **Server Population Management**
Maintain active appearance during off-peak hours: ```yaml body: enabled: true pushable: false # Prevent griefing damageable: false # Immortal population bots fake-chat: enabled: true interval: min: 120 max: 600 swap: enabled: true session: { min: 300, max: 1800 } absence: { min: 30, max: 120 } ```
### **Minigame Lobby NPCs**
Static, interactive NPCs for game lobbies: ```yaml body: enabled: true pushable: false damageable: false head-ai: enabled: true look-range: 5.0 chunk-loading: enabled: true radius: 1 ```
### **Roleplay Town Inhabitants**
Realistic townsfolk with chat activity: ```yaml fake-chat: enabled: true interval: { min: 60, max: 300 } activity-variation: true head-ai: enabled: true look-range: 6.0 body: pushable: true ```
---
## **Troubleshooting**
### вқҢ **Common Issues**
**Bots not rotating heads:** - вң… Check `head-ai.enabled: true` - вң… Verify `head-ai.look-range` covers the player distance - вң… Ensure `body.enabled: true`
**Performance problems:** - вң… Reduce `limits.max-bots` - вң… Set `chunk-loading.enabled: false` - вң… Increase `chunk-loading.update-interval` - вң… Monitor with `/fpp stats --detailed`
**Physics not working:** - вң… Verify `body.pushable: true` - вң… Check `collision.walk-radius` and `collision.walk-strength` values - вң… Ensure physical bodies are enabled (`body.enabled: true`)
### пёҸ **Diagnostic Commands**
``` /fpp stats --detailed # Performance and behavior stats /fpp info <botname> # Individual bot status /fpp freeze <botname> # Pause a specific bot's AI /fpp list --frozen # Check frozen bot status ```
---
## **Related Documentation**
- **[Skin System](Skin-System.md)** вҖ” Bot visual appearance - **[Fake Chat](Fake-Chat.md)** вҖ” Chat AI configuration - **[Swap System](Swap-System.md)** вҖ” Session rotation details - **[Configuration](Configuration.md)** вҖ” All behaviour settings - **[FAQ](FAQ.md#performance-issues)** вҖ” Optimization tips
---
** Master bot behaviour to create the most realistic fake players!**
