option.value: /pom (snapshot).xml

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 http://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd"> <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>
<groupId>me.bill</groupId> <artifactId>fpp</artifactId> <version>1.6.0</version> <packaging>jar</packaging>
<name>fpp</name>
<properties> <java.version>21</java.version> <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding> <!-- Server plugins folder вҖ” override with -Ddeploy.dir=... or edit here --> <deploy.dir>${user.home}/Desktop/dmc/plugins</deploy.dir> <!-- Set to true to skip ProGuard obfuscation and deploy non-obfuscated jar --> <skip.proguard>false</skip.proguard> <!-- Local build output folder inside the project root --> <build.output.dir>${basedir}/build</build.output.dir> <!-- Java executable used to run ProGuard. The bundled proguard.jar has a runtime incompatibility with JDK 25 вҖ” it crashes with NoClassDefFoundError: org/json/JSONException when run under JDK 25. Use JDK 17 or 21 to run ProGuard. Override on the command line: -Dproguard.java="C:\path\to\java.exe" --> <proguard.java>C:/Program Files/Java/jdk-17/bin/java.exe</proguard.java> <!-- JDK home for the ProGuard execution JDK (must match proguard.java above). ProGuard preverification needs java.base.jmod to resolve java.lang.Object for LUB (least-upper-bound) computation in StackMapTable generation. Override with -Dproguard.jdk.home=... if your JDK 17 is elsewhere. --> <proguard.jdk.home>C:/Program Files/Java/jdk-17</proguard.jdk.home> </properties>
<build> <defaultGoal>clean package</defaultGoal> <plugins> <plugin> <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId> <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId> <version>3.13.0</version> <configuration> <release>21</release> <showDeprecation>true</showDeprecation> </configuration> </plugin> <plugin> <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId> <artifactId>maven-shade-plugin</artifactId> <version>3.5.3</version> <executions> <execution> <phase>package</phase> <goals> <goal>shade</goal> </goals> <configuration> <minimizeJar>false</minimizeJar> <createDependencyReducedPom>false</createDependencyReducedPom> <artifactSet> <includes> <include>org.xerial:sqlite-jdbc</include> <include>com.mysql:mysql-connector-j</include> <!-- FastStats is NOT shaded вҖ” it is bundled as raw jar resources (src/main/resources/faststats/) and loaded via URLClassLoader at runtime to avoid shade-relocation class-loading issues. --> </includes> </artifactSet> <!-- Exclude MANIFEST.MF and duplicate service descriptors from shaded dependency JARs so the shade plugin doesn't emit overlap warnings. The project's own MANIFEST.MF (built by maven-jar-plugin) is kept. --> <filters> <filter> <artifact>org.xerial:sqlite-jdbc</artifact> <excludes> <exclude>META-INF/MANIFEST.MF</exclude> <exclude>META-INF/services/java.sql.Driver</exclude> </excludes> </filter> <filter> <artifact>com.mysql:mysql-connector-j</artifact> <excludes> <exclude>META-INF/MANIFEST.MF</exclude> <exclude>META-INF/services/java.sql.Driver</exclude> </excludes> </filter> </filters> <transformers> <!-- Merges MANIFEST.MF entries instead of silently dropping duplicates --> <transformer implementation="org.apache.maven.plugins.shade.resource.ManifestResourceTransformer"/> <!-- Merges META-INF/services/* (e.g. java.sql.Driver) across shaded JARs --> <transformer implementation="org.apache.maven.plugins.shade.resource.ServicesResourceTransformer"/> </transformers> </configuration> </execution> </executions> </plugin> <plugin> <groupId>org.codehaus.mojo</groupId> <artifactId>exec-maven-plugin</artifactId> <version>3.1.0</version> <executions> <execution> <id>proguard-obfuscation</id> <phase>package</phase> <goals> <goal>exec</goal> </goals> <configuration> <skip>${skip.proguard}</skip> <!-- Use JDK 17 to run ProGuard: the bundled proguard.jar supports class files up to version 65 (Java 21). JDK 25 jmods are version 69 and would cause a parse error. JDK 17 jmods are version 61 вҖ” safe. Override with -Dproguard.java=<path> if JDK 17 is elsewhere. --> <executable>${proguard.java}</executable> <arguments> <!-- Use -cp instead of -jar so we can include org.json.jar (required by ProGuard's internal jsonprinter utility). --> <argument>-cp</argument> <argument>${basedir}/libs/proguard/proguard.jar${path.separator}${basedir}/libs/proguard/org.json.jar</argument> <argument>proguard.ProGuard</argument> <argument>@${basedir}/libs/proguard/fpp.conf</argument> <!-- Inject java.base.jmod as a library jar so ProGuard can resolve java.lang.Object during preverification (LUB computation for StackMapTable generation). Without this, ProGuard throws IncompleteClassHierarchyException even for non-MySQL classes because Object itself is unknown. --> <argument>-libraryjars</argument> <argument>${proguard.jdk.home}/jmods/java.base.jmod(!**.jar;!module-info.class)</argument> <!-- MySQL and SQLite are filtered OUT of -injars so ProGuard's preverification never processes their internal methods (they reference classes outside our library set and would crash with IncompleteClassHierarchyException). They are merged back into the obfuscated JAR by the antrun step below. --> <argument>-injars</argument> <argument>${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}.jar(!com/mysql/**,!org/sqlite/**)</argument> <argument>-outjars</argument> <argument>${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}-obfuscated.jar</argument> </arguments> </configuration> </execution> </executions> </plugin> <!-- Auto-deploy: copy obfuscated JAR to server plugins folder after ProGuard --> <plugin> <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId> <artifactId>maven-antrun-plugin</artifactId> <version>3.1.0</version> <executions> <execution> <id>deploy-obfuscated-jar</id> <phase>package</phase> <goals> <goal>run</goal> </goals> <configuration> <target> <!-- Merge MySQL + SQLite classes back into the obfuscated JAR. They were excluded from -injars so ProGuard's preverification never tries to analyse their internal method bodies (which reference JDK types outside our library set). Their original class files (already containing correct StackMapTable from their own compilation) are merged verbatim from the pre-ProGuard JAR. --> <zip destfile="${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}-obfuscated.jar" update="true"> <zipfileset src="${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}.jar" includes="com/mysql/**,org/sqlite/**"/> </zip>
<!-- Copy to project-local build/ folder --> <mkdir dir="${build.output.dir}" /> <copy file="${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}-obfuscated.jar" tofile="${build.output.dir}/fpp.jar" overwrite="true" failonerror="false" /> <copy file="${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}.jar" tofile="${build.output.dir}/fpp.jar" overwrite="false" failonerror="false" /> <echo level="info" message="[FPP] Build output: ${build.output.dir}/fpp.jar" />
<!-- Also deploy to server plugins folder --> <mkdir dir="${deploy.dir}" /> <!-- Primary: deploy obfuscated jar (produced by ProGuard) --> <copy file="${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}-obfuscated.jar" tofile="${deploy.dir}/fpp.jar" overwrite="true" failonerror="false" /> <!-- Fallback: deploy non-obfuscated jar when ProGuard is skipped/missing --> <copy file="${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}.jar" tofile="${deploy.dir}/fpp.jar" overwrite="false" failonerror="false" /> <echo level="info" message="[FPP] Deployed JAR to: ${deploy.dir}/fpp.jar" /> </target> </configuration> </execution> </executions> </plugin> </plugins> <resources> <!-- Regular resources: YAML, properties, etc. вҖ” text filtering enabled --> <resource> <directory>src/main/resources</directory> <filtering>true</filtering> <excludes> <!-- Binary jar files must NOT be text-filtered --> <exclude>faststats/**</exclude> </excludes> </resource> <!-- FastStats bundled jars вҖ” binary, no filtering --> <resource> <directory>src/main/resources</directory> <filtering>false</filtering> <includes> <include>faststats/**</include> </includes> </resource> </resources> </build>
<repositories> <repository> <id>papermc-repo</id> <url>https://repo.papermc.io/repository/maven-public/</url> </repository> <repository> <id>central</id> <url>https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2</url> </repository> <repository> <id>placeholderapi-repo</id> <url>https://repo.extendedclip.com/content/repositories/placeholderapi/</url> </repository> <repository> <id>enginehub</id> <url>https://maven.enginehub.org/repo/</url> </repository> <repository> <id>faststats-releases</id> <url>https://repo.faststats.dev/releases</url> </repository> <!-- guardsquare removed from <repositories>: ProGuard is run via a local JAR (exec-maven-plugin), not as a Maven dependency. It was causing spurious "No such host" warnings for every SNAPSHOT artifact resolution. --> </repositories>
<pluginRepositories> <pluginRepository> <id>guardsquare</id> <url>https://repository.guardsquare.com/content/repositories/releases</url> </pluginRepository> </pluginRepositories>
<dependencies> <!-- Paper public API вҖ” the only compile-time dependency needed --> <dependency> <groupId>io.papermc.paper</groupId> <artifactId>paper-api</artifactId> <version>1.21.11-R0.1-SNAPSHOT</version> <scope>provided</scope> </dependency>
<!-- Netty вҖ” bundled with Paper, needed at compile time for network classes --> <dependency> <groupId>io.netty</groupId> <artifactId>netty-all</artifactId> <version>4.1.97.Final</version> <scope>provided</scope> </dependency>
<!-- True Mojang-mapped server JAR вҖ” needed for FakeServerGamePacketListenerImpl which extends ServerGamePacketListenerImpl with proper Mojang class+method names. This JAR is already installed in the local Maven repo. NOTE: paper-mojmap was removed because it has Spigot-obfuscated methods that shadow Entity/ServerCommonPacketListenerImpl from this JAR on the classpath. --> <dependency> <groupId>io.papermc.paper</groupId> <artifactId>paper-server</artifactId> <version>1.21.11-R0.1-SNAPSHOT</version> <classifier>mojang-mapped</classifier> <scope>provided</scope> </dependency>
<!-- SQLite JDBC вҖ” shaded in for local database fallback --> <dependency> <groupId>org.xerial</groupId> <artifactId>sqlite-jdbc</artifactId> <version>3.47.1.0</version> <scope>compile</scope> </dependency>
<!-- MySQL Connector вҖ” provided by the server or shaded in as fallback --> <!-- If your server doesn't bundle it, change scope to compile --> <dependency> <groupId>com.mysql</groupId> <artifactId>mysql-connector-j</artifactId> <version>8.3.0</version> <scope>compile</scope> <exclusions> <!-- Exclude protobuf to avoid conflicts --> <exclusion> <groupId>com.google.protobuf</groupId> <artifactId>protobuf-java</artifactId> </exclusion> </exclusions> </dependency>
<!-- LuckPerms API (provided by the server at runtime) --> <dependency> <groupId>net.luckperms</groupId> <artifactId>api</artifactId> <version>5.5</version> <scope>provided</scope> </dependency>
<!-- PlaceholderAPI вҖ” soft optional; provided by server/plugin environment --> <dependency> <groupId>me.clip</groupId> <artifactId>placeholderapi</artifactId> <version>2.11.6</version> <scope>provided</scope> </dependency>
<!-- WorldGuard API вҖ” soft optional; provided by server/plugin environment --> <dependency> <groupId>com.sk89q.worldguard</groupId> <artifactId>worldguard-bukkit</artifactId> <version>7.0.12</version> <scope>provided</scope> </dependency>
<!-- FastStats вҖ” anonymous usage analytics (shaded + relocated) --> <dependency> <groupId>dev.faststats.metrics</groupId> <artifactId>bukkit</artifactId> <version>0.18.1</version> <scope>compile</scope> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>dev.faststats.metrics</groupId> <artifactId>core</artifactId> <version>0.18.1</version> <scope>compile</scope> </dependency> <!-- Explicitly add safe version to override vulnerable transitive dependency --> <dependency> <groupId>org.apache.commons</groupId> <artifactId>commons-lang3</artifactId> <version>3.18.0</version> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>com.google.code.gson</groupId> <artifactId>gson</artifactId> <version>2.13.2</version> <scope>provided</scope> </dependency> </dependencies> </project>

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 http://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd"> <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>
<groupId>me.bill</groupId> <artifactId>fpp</artifactId> <version>1.6.0</version> <packaging>jar</packaging>
<name>fpp</name>
<properties> <java.version>21</java.version> <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding> <!-- Server plugins folder вҖ” override with -Ddeploy.dir=... or edit here --> <deploy.dir>${user.home}/Desktop/dmc/plugins</deploy.dir> <!-- Set to true to skip ProGuard obfuscation and deploy non-obfuscated jar --> <skip.proguard>false</skip.proguard> <!-- Local build output folder inside the project root --> <build.output.dir>${basedir}/build</build.output.dir> <!-- Java executable used to run ProGuard. The bundled proguard.jar has a runtime incompatibility with JDK 25 вҖ” it crashes with NoClassDefFoundError: org/json/JSONException when run under JDK 25. Use JDK 17 or 21 to run ProGuard. Override on the command line: -Dproguard.java="C:\path\to\java.exe" --> <proguard.java>C:/Program Files/Java/jdk-17/bin/java.exe</proguard.java> <!-- JDK home for the ProGuard execution JDK (must match proguard.java above). ProGuard preverification needs java.base.jmod to resolve java.lang.Object for LUB (least-upper-bound) computation in StackMapTable generation. Override with -Dproguard.jdk.home=... if your JDK 17 is elsewhere. --> <proguard.jdk.home>C:/Program Files/Java/jdk-17</proguard.jdk.home> </properties>
<build> <defaultGoal>clean package</defaultGoal> <plugins> <plugin> <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId> <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId> <version>3.13.0</version> <configuration> <release>21</release> <showDeprecation>true</showDeprecation> </configuration> </plugin> <plugin> <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId> <artifactId>maven-shade-plugin</artifactId> <version>3.5.3</version> <executions> <execution> <phase>package</phase> <goals> <goal>shade</goal> </goals> <configuration> <minimizeJar>false</minimizeJar> <createDependencyReducedPom>false</createDependencyReducedPom> <artifactSet> <includes> <include>org.xerial:sqlite-jdbc</include> <include>com.mysql:mysql-connector-j</include> <!-- FastStats is NOT shaded вҖ” it is bundled as raw jar resources (src/main/resources/faststats/) and loaded via URLClassLoader at runtime to avoid shade-relocation class-loading issues. --> </includes> </artifactSet> <!-- Exclude MANIFEST.MF and duplicate service descriptors from shaded dependency JARs so the shade plugin doesn't emit overlap warnings. The project's own MANIFEST.MF (built by maven-jar-plugin) is kept. --> <filters> <filter> <artifact>org.xerial:sqlite-jdbc</artifact> <excludes> <exclude>META-INF/MANIFEST.MF</exclude> <exclude>META-INF/services/java.sql.Driver</exclude> </excludes> </filter> <filter> <artifact>com.mysql:mysql-connector-j</artifact> <excludes> <exclude>META-INF/MANIFEST.MF</exclude> <exclude>META-INF/services/java.sql.Driver</exclude> </excludes> </filter> </filters> <transformers> <!-- Merges MANIFEST.MF entries instead of silently dropping duplicates --> <transformer implementation="org.apache.maven.plugins.shade.resource.ManifestResourceTransformer"/> <!-- Merges META-INF/services/* (e.g. java.sql.Driver) across shaded JARs --> <transformer implementation="org.apache.maven.plugins.shade.resource.ServicesResourceTransformer"/> </transformers> </configuration> </execution> </executions> </plugin> <plugin> <groupId>org.codehaus.mojo</groupId> <artifactId>exec-maven-plugin</artifactId> <version>3.1.0</version> <executions> <execution> <id>proguard-obfuscation</id> <phase>package</phase> <goals> <goal>exec</goal> </goals> <configuration> <skip>${skip.proguard}</skip> <!-- Use JDK 17 to run ProGuard: the bundled proguard.jar supports class files up to version 65 (Java 21). JDK 25 jmods are version 69 and would cause a parse error. JDK 17 jmods are version 61 вҖ” safe. Override with -Dproguard.java=<path> if JDK 17 is elsewhere. --> <executable>${proguard.java}</executable> <arguments> <!-- Use -cp instead of -jar so we can include org.json.jar (required by ProGuard's internal jsonprinter utility). --> <argument>-cp</argument> <argument>${basedir}/libs/proguard/proguard.jar${path.separator}${basedir}/libs/proguard/org.json.jar</argument> <argument>proguard.ProGuard</argument> <argument>@${basedir}/libs/proguard/fpp.conf</argument> <!-- Inject java.base.jmod as a library jar so ProGuard can resolve java.lang.Object during preverification (LUB computation for StackMapTable generation). Without this, ProGuard throws IncompleteClassHierarchyException even for non-MySQL classes because Object itself is unknown. --> <argument>-libraryjars</argument> <argument>${proguard.jdk.home}/jmods/java.base.jmod(!**.jar;!module-info.class)</argument> <!-- MySQL and SQLite are filtered OUT of -injars so ProGuard's preverification never processes their internal methods (they reference classes outside our library set and would crash with IncompleteClassHierarchyException). They are merged back into the obfuscated JAR by the antrun step below. --> <argument>-injars</argument> <argument>${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}.jar(!com/mysql/**,!org/sqlite/**)</argument> <argument>-outjars</argument> <argument>${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}-obfuscated.jar</argument> </arguments> </configuration> </execution> </executions> </plugin> <!-- Auto-deploy: copy obfuscated JAR to server plugins folder after ProGuard --> <plugin> <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId> <artifactId>maven-antrun-plugin</artifactId> <version>3.1.0</version> <executions> <execution> <id>deploy-obfuscated-jar</id> <phase>package</phase> <goals> <goal>run</goal> </goals> <configuration> <target> <!-- Merge MySQL + SQLite classes back into the obfuscated JAR. They were excluded from -injars so ProGuard's preverification never tries to analyse their internal method bodies (which reference JDK types outside our library set). Their original class files (already containing correct StackMapTable from their own compilation) are merged verbatim from the pre-ProGuard JAR. --> <zip destfile="${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}-obfuscated.jar" update="true"> <zipfileset src="${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}.jar" includes="com/mysql/**,org/sqlite/**"/> </zip>
<!-- Copy to project-local build/ folder --> <mkdir dir="${build.output.dir}" /> <copy file="${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}-obfuscated.jar" tofile="${build.output.dir}/fpp.jar" overwrite="true" failonerror="false" /> <copy file="${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}.jar" tofile="${build.output.dir}/fpp.jar" overwrite="false" failonerror="false" /> <echo level="info" message="[FPP] Build output: ${build.output.dir}/fpp.jar" />
<!-- Also deploy to server plugins folder --> <mkdir dir="${deploy.dir}" /> <!-- Primary: deploy obfuscated jar (produced by ProGuard) --> <copy file="${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}-obfuscated.jar" tofile="${deploy.dir}/fpp.jar" overwrite="true" failonerror="false" /> <!-- Fallback: deploy non-obfuscated jar when ProGuard is skipped/missing --> <copy file="${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}.jar" tofile="${deploy.dir}/fpp.jar" overwrite="false" failonerror="false" /> <echo level="info" message="[FPP] Deployed JAR to: ${deploy.dir}/fpp.jar" /> </target> </configuration> </execution> </executions> </plugin> </plugins> <resources> <!-- Regular resources: YAML, properties, etc. вҖ” text filtering enabled --> <resource> <directory>src/main/resources</directory> <filtering>true</filtering> <excludes> <!-- Binary jar files must NOT be text-filtered --> <exclude>faststats/**</exclude> </excludes> </resource> <!-- FastStats bundled jars вҖ” binary, no filtering --> <resource> <directory>src/main/resources</directory> <filtering>false</filtering> <includes> <include>faststats/**</include> </includes> </resource> </resources> </build>
<repositories> <repository> <id>papermc-repo</id> <url>https://repo.papermc.io/repository/maven-public/</url> </repository> <repository> <id>central</id> <url>https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2</url> </repository> <repository> <id>placeholderapi-repo</id> <url>https://repo.extendedclip.com/content/repositories/placeholderapi/</url> </repository> <repository> <id>enginehub</id> <url>https://maven.enginehub.org/repo/</url> </repository> <repository> <id>faststats-releases</id> <url>https://repo.faststats.dev/releases</url> </repository> <!-- guardsquare removed from <repositories>: ProGuard is run via a local JAR (exec-maven-plugin), not as a Maven dependency. It was causing spurious "No such host" warnings for every SNAPSHOT artifact resolution. --> </repositories>
<pluginRepositories> <pluginRepository> <id>guardsquare</id> <url>https://repository.guardsquare.com/content/repositories/releases</url> </pluginRepository> </pluginRepositories>
<dependencies> <!-- Paper public API вҖ” the only compile-time dependency needed --> <dependency> <groupId>io.papermc.paper</groupId> <artifactId>paper-api</artifactId> <version>1.21.11-R0.1-SNAPSHOT</version> <scope>provided</scope> </dependency>
<!-- Netty вҖ” bundled with Paper, needed at compile time for network classes --> <dependency> <groupId>io.netty</groupId> <artifactId>netty-all</artifactId> <version>4.1.97.Final</version> <scope>provided</scope> </dependency>
<!-- True Mojang-mapped server JAR вҖ” needed for FakeServerGamePacketListenerImpl which extends ServerGamePacketListenerImpl with proper Mojang class+method names. This JAR is already installed in the local Maven repo. NOTE: paper-mojmap was removed because it has Spigot-obfuscated methods that shadow Entity/ServerCommonPacketListenerImpl from this JAR on the classpath. --> <dependency> <groupId>io.papermc.paper</groupId> <artifactId>paper-server</artifactId> <version>1.21.11-R0.1-SNAPSHOT</version> <classifier>mojang-mapped</classifier> <scope>provided</scope> </dependency>
<!-- Mojang DataFixerUpper вҖ” required by paper-server for type annotations --> <dependency> <groupId>com.mojang</groupId> <artifactId>datafixerupper</artifactId> <version>8.0.16</version> <scope>provided</scope> </dependency>
<!-- SQLite JDBC вҖ” shaded in for local database fallback --> <dependency> <groupId>org.xerial</groupId> <artifactId>sqlite-jdbc</artifactId> <version>3.47.1.0</version> <scope>compile</scope> </dependency>
<!-- MySQL Connector вҖ” provided by the server or shaded in as fallback --> <!-- If your server doesn't bundle it, change scope to compile --> <dependency> <groupId>com.mysql</groupId> <artifactId>mysql-connector-j</artifactId> <version>8.3.0</version> <scope>compile</scope> <exclusions> <!-- Exclude protobuf to avoid conflicts --> <exclusion> <groupId>com.google.protobuf</groupId> <artifactId>protobuf-java</artifactId> </exclusion> </exclusions> </dependency>
<!-- LuckPerms API (provided by the server at runtime) --> <dependency> <groupId>net.luckperms</groupId> <artifactId>api</artifactId> <version>5.5</version> <scope>provided</scope> </dependency>
<!-- PlaceholderAPI вҖ” soft optional; provided by server/plugin environment --> <dependency> <groupId>me.clip</groupId> <artifactId>placeholderapi</artifactId> <version>2.11.6</version> <scope>provided</scope> </dependency>
<!-- WorldGuard API вҖ” soft optional; provided by server/plugin environment --> <dependency> <groupId>com.sk89q.worldguard</groupId> <artifactId>worldguard-bukkit</artifactId> <version>7.0.12</version> <scope>provided</scope> </dependency>
<!-- FastStats вҖ” anonymous usage analytics (shaded + relocated) --> <dependency> <groupId>dev.faststats.metrics</groupId> <artifactId>bukkit</artifactId> <version>0.18.1</version> <scope>compile</scope> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>dev.faststats.metrics</groupId> <artifactId>core</artifactId> <version>0.18.1</version> <scope>compile</scope> </dependency> <!-- Explicitly add safe version to override vulnerable transitive dependency --> <dependency> <groupId>org.apache.commons</groupId> <artifactId>commons-lang3</artifactId> <version>3.18.0</version> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>com.google.code.gson</groupId> <artifactId>gson</artifactId> <version>2.13.2</version> <scope>provided</scope> </dependency> </dependencies> </project>
